1.author: Ahmed Ali
1.text: BLESSED IS HE who revealed the Criterion (of right and wrong) to His votary that it may be a warning for the world, --

1.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
1.text: Most Auspicious is He Who has sent down the Furqan (the Criterion – the Holy Qur’an) upon His chosen bondman for him to be a Herald of Warning to the entire world. (Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him – is the Prophet towards all mankind.)

1.author: Arberry
1.text: Blessed be He who has sent down the Salvation upon His servant, that he may be a warner to all beings;

1.author: Daryabadi
1.text: Blest be He who hath revealed the Criterion unto His bondman thathe may be unto the worlds a warner.

1.author: Hilali & Khan
1.text: Blessed be He Who sent down the criterion (of right and wrong, i.e. this Quran) to His slave (Muhammad SAW) that he may be a warner to the 'Alamin (mankind and jinns).

1.author: Itani
1.text: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His servant, to be a warning to humanity.

1.author: Maududi
1.text: Highly blessed is He, Who has sent down Al-Furqan, to His servant so that it may be a warner to all mankind:

1.author: Mubarakpuri
1.text: Blessed be He Who sent down (Nazzala) the criterion to His servant that he may be a warner to all nations.

1.author: Pickthall
1.text: Blessed is He Who hath revealed unto His slave the Criterion (of right and wrong), that he may be a warner to the peoples.

1.author: Qarai
1.text: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion to His servant that he may be a warner to all the nations.

1.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
1.text: Blessed is He who has sent down the Criterion to His worshiper (Prophet Muhammad), that he is a warner to all mankind;

1.author: Saheeh International
1.text: Blessed is He who sent down the Criterion upon His Servant that he may be to the worlds a warner -

1.author: Sarwar
1.text: Blessed is He who has revealed the criteria (for discerning truth from falsehood) to His servant so that He could warn mankind.

1.author: Shakir
1.text: Blessed is He Who sent down the Furqan upon His servant that he may be a warner to the nations;

1.author: Wahiduddin Khan
1.text: Blessed be He who has revealed the criterion [the Quran] to His servant that he may warn the nations.

1.author: Yusuf Ali
1.text: Blessed is He who sent down the criterion to His servant, that it may be an admonition to all creatures;-

1.author: Transliteration
1.text: Tabaraka allathee nazzala alfurqana AAala AAabdihi liyakoona lilAAalameena natheeran

2.author: Ahmed Ali
2.text: He to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, who has neither begotten a son nor has He a partner in His kingdom, (who) created everything and determined its exact measure.

2.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
2.text: The One to Whom belongs the kingship of the heavens and of the earth, and has not chosen a child, and has no partner in His kingship, and He has created everything and kept it in proper measure.

2.author: Arberry
2.text: to whom belongs the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth; and He has not taken to Him a son, and He has no associate in the Kingdom; and He created every thing, then He ordained it very exactly.

2.author: Daryabadi
2.text: He whose is the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and who hath not taken a son, and for whom there is not an associate in the dominion, and who hath created everything, and measured it according to a measurement.

2.author: Hilali & Khan
2.text: He to Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and Who has begotten no son (children or offspring) and for Whom there is no partner in the dominion. He has created everything, and has measured it exactly according to its due measurements.

2.author: Itani
2.text: He to whom belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, who took to Himself no son, who never had a partner in His kingship; who created everything and determined its measure.

2.author: Maududi
2.text: He to Whom belongs the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth: Who has begotten no son: Who has no partner in His Sovereignty: Who created each and every thing and then ordained its destiny.

2.author: Mubarakpuri
2.text: He to Whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth, and Who has begotten no son and for Whom there is no partner in the dominion. He has created everything, and has measured it exactly according to its due measurements.

2.author: Pickthall
2.text: He unto Whom belongeth the Sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, He hath chosen no son nor hath He any partner in the Sovereignty. He hath created everything and hath meted out for it a measure.

2.author: Qarai
2.text: He, to whom belongs the sovereignty of the heavens and the earth, and who did not take any offspring, nor has He any partner in sovereignty, and He created everything and determined it in a precise measure.

2.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
2.text: to whom the Kingdom of the heavens and the earth belongs, who has not taken a son, nor does He have an associate in the Kingdom, and He created everything, then He ordained it very precisely.

2.author: Saheeh International
2.text: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth and who has not taken a son and has not had a partner in dominion and has created each thing and determined it with [precise] determination.

2.author: Sarwar
2.text: To Him belongs the kingdom of the heavens and the earth. He has not begotten any sons, nor does He have any partner in His kingdom. He has created all things with precisely accurate planning.

2.author: Shakir
2.text: He, Whose is the kingdom of the heavens and the earth, and Who did not take to Himself a son, and Who has no associate in the kingdom, and Who created everything, then ordained for it a measure.

2.author: Wahiduddin Khan
2.text: Sovereign of the heavens and the earth, who has begotten no children and who has no partner in His sovereignty, it is He who has created all things and measured them out precisely.

2.author: Yusuf Ali
2.text: He to whom belongs the dominion of the heavens and the earth: no son has He begotten, nor has He a partner in His dominion: it is He who created all things, and ordered them in due proportions.

2.author: Transliteration
2.text: Allathee lahu mulku alssamawati waalardi walam yattakhith waladan walam yakun lahu shareekun fee almulki wakhalaqa kulla shayin faqaddarahu taqdeeran

3.author: Ahmed Ali
3.text: Yet they choose apart from Him gods who have not created any thing and have themselves been created, who possess no power over their loss or gain, or their death or life or being raised to life again.

3.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
3.text: And people have appointed other Gods besides Him, those who do not create anything but are themselves created, and those who do not have the power to harm or benefit themselves, and who do not have any authority over death or life nor over resurrection.

3.author: Arberry
3.text: Yet they have taken to them gods, apart from Him, that create nothing and themselves are created, and have no power to hurt or profit themselves, no power of death or life or raising up.

3.author: Daryabadi
3.text: And they have taken beside Him gods creating not aught and are themselves created, owning for themselves neither hurt nor benefit, and owning not death nor life nor resurrection.

3.author: Hilali & Khan
3.text: Yet they have taken besides Him other aliha (gods) that created nothing but are themselves created, and possess neither hurt nor benefit for themselves, and possess no power (of causing) death, nor (of giving) life, nor of raising the dead.

3.author: Itani
3.text: And yet, instead of Him, they produce for themselves gods that create nothing, but are themselves created; that have no power to harm or benefit themselves; and no power over life, death, or resurrection.

3.author: Maududi
3.text: Yet the people have set up, besides Him, deities, who do not create anything but are themselves created: who can neither harm nor help even themselves: who have no power over life or death, nor can they raise up the dead.

3.author: Mubarakpuri
3.text: Yet they have taken besides Him other gods who created nothing but are themselves created, and possess neither harm nor benefit for themselves, and possess no power (of causing) death, nor (of giving) life, nor of raising the dead.

3.author: Pickthall
3.text: Yet they choose beside Him other gods who create naught but are themselves created, and possess not hurt nor profit for themselves, and possess not death nor life, nor power to raise the dead.

3.author: Qarai
3.text: Yet they have taken gods besides Him who create nothing and have themselves been created, and who have no control over their own harm or benefit and have no control over [their own] death, life, or resurrection.

3.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
3.text: Yet they worship, other than Him, gods which cannot create anything and were themselves created. They own neither harm nor benefit for themselves, neither do they own death nor life, nor a resurrection.

3.author: Saheeh International
3.text: But they have taken besides Him gods which create nothing, while they are created, and possess not for themselves any harm or benefit and possess not [power to cause] death or life or resurrection.

3.author: Sarwar
3.text: Yet they have chosen for themselves other deities besides Him, who do not create anything but rather are themselves created, who have no power over their own benefits, or trouble, and who have no control over life, death, and resurrection.

3.author: Shakir
3.text: And they have taken besides Him gods, who do not create anything while they are themselves created, and they control not for themselves any harm or profit, and they control not death nor life, nor raising (the dead) to life.

3.author: Wahiduddin Khan
3.text: Yet they have taken, besides Him, deities who create nothing and are themselves created, and who have no power to harm, or benefit themselves and who control neither death, nor life, nor resurrection.

3.author: Yusuf Ali
3.text: Yet have they taken, besides him, gods that can create nothing but are themselves created; that have no control of hurt or good to themselves; nor can they control death nor life nor resurrection.

3.author: Transliteration
3.text: Waittakhathoo min doonihi alihatan la yakhluqoona shayan wahum yukhlaqoona wala yamlikoona lianfusihim darran wala nafAAan wala yamlikoona mawtan wala hayatan wala nushooran

4.author: Ahmed Ali
4.text: Yet the unbelievers say: "This is nothing but a lie he has concocted in which others have aided him." They have come down to mischief and lies.

4.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
4.text: And the disbelievers said, “This is nothing but a slur he has fabricated and others have helped him upon it”; so indeed the disbelievers have stooped to injustice and lie.

4.author: Arberry
4.text: The unbelievers say, 'This is naught but a calumny he has forged, and other folk have helped him to it.' So they have committed wrong and falsehood.

4.author: Daryabadi
4.text: And those who disbelieve, say. this is naught but a falsehood thathe hath fabricated and there have assisted him therein other people. so surelythey have aimed at a wrong and a fraud.

4.author: Hilali & Khan
4.text: Those who disbelieve say: "This (the Quran) is nothing but a lie that he (Muhammad SAW) has invented, and others have helped him at it, so that they have produced an unjust wrong (thing) and a lie."

4.author: Itani
4.text: Those who disbelieve say, “This is nothing but a lie that he made up, and others have helped him at it.” They have committed an injustice and a perjury.

4.author: Maududi
4.text: Those who have rejected the Message of the Prophet, say, "This (Al-Furgan) is a forgery which this man himself has devised, and some others have helped him at it. " What a cruel injustice and an impudent lie!

4.author: Mubarakpuri
4.text: Those who disbelieve say: "This is nothing but a lie that he has invented, and others have helped him in it." In fact, they have produced an unjust wrong and a lie.

4.author: Pickthall
4.text: Those who disbelieve say: This is naught but a lie that he hath invented, and other folk have helped him with it, so that they have produced a slander and a lie.

4.author: Qarai
4.text: The faithless say, ‘This is nothing but a lie that he has fabricated, and other people have abetted him in it.’ Thus they have certainly come out with wrongdoing and falsehood.

4.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
4.text: The unbelievers say: 'This is but a falsehood he has forged? another nation has helped him' So they have come with wrong and falsehood.

4.author: Saheeh International
4.text: And those who disbelieve say, "This [Qur'an] is not except a falsehood he invented, and another people assisted him in it." But they have committed an injustice and a lie.

4.author: Sarwar
4.text: The unbelievers say, "This (Quran) is no more than a slanderous statement which he (Muhammad), with the help of some other people, has falsely invented." Certainly, this statement is unjust and sinful.

4.author: Shakir
4.text: And those who disbelieve say: This is nothing but a lie which he has forged, and other people have helped him at it; so indeed they have done injustice and (uttered) a falsehood.

4.author: Wahiduddin Khan
4.text: Those who deny the truth say, "This is only a forgery of his own invention in which others have helped him." What they say is unjust and false.

4.author: Yusuf Ali
4.text: But the misbelievers say: "Naught is this but a lie which he has forged, and others have helped him at it." In truth it is they who have put forward an iniquity and a falsehood.

4.author: Transliteration
4.text: Waqala allatheena kafaroo in hatha illa ifkun iftarahu waaAAanahu AAalayhi qawmun akharoona faqad jaoo thulman wazooran

5.author: Ahmed Ali
5.text: And they say: "These are fables of antiquity he has invented, which are dictated to him morning and evening."

5.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
5.text: And they said, “These are stories of the former people, which he has written down, so they are read to him morning and evening.”

5.author: Arberry
5.text: They say, 'Fairy-tales of the ancients that he has had written down, so that they are recited to him at the dawn and in the evening.'

5.author: Daryabadi
5.text: And they say: fables of the ancients which he hath had written down, so they are dictated unto him, morning and evening.

5.author: Hilali & Khan
5.text: And they say: "Tales of the ancients, which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon."

5.author: Itani
5.text: And they say, “Tales of the ancients; he wrote them down; they are dictated to him morning and evening.”

5.author: Maududi
5.text: They say, "These things are the writings of the ancients which he has got copied down for himself, and then these are recited to him in the morning and evening.''

5.author: Mubarakpuri
5.text: And they say: "Tales of the ancients which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon. "

5.author: Pickthall
5.text: And they say: Fables of the men of old which he hath had written down so that they are dictated to him morn and evening.

5.author: Qarai
5.text: They say, ‘He has taken down myths of the ancients, and they are dictated to him morning and evening.’

5.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
5.text: They say: 'He has written tales of the ancients, they are recited to him at dawn and at the evening'

5.author: Saheeh International
5.text: And they say, "Legends of the former peoples which he has written down, and they are dictated to him morning and afternoon."

5.author: Sarwar
5.text: They have also said, "It, (the Quran), is only ancient legends, which were written down while they were dictated to him in the mornings and the evenings".

5.author: Shakir
5.text: And they say: The stories of the ancients-- he has got them written-- so these are read out to him morning and evening.

5.author: Wahiduddin Khan
5.text: They say, "It is just fables of the ancients, which he has had written down. They are dictated to him morning and evening."

5.author: Yusuf Ali
5.text: And they say: "Tales of the ancients, which he has caused to be written: and they are dictated before him morning and evening."

5.author: Transliteration
5.text: Waqaloo asateeru alawwaleena iktatabaha fahiya tumla AAalayhi bukratan waaseelan

6.author: Ahmed Ali
6.text: Say: "He who knows the secrets of the heavens and the earth has revealed it to me; and He is surely forgiving and kind."

6.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
6.text: Proclaim (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him), “It is sent down by Him Who knows all the secrets of the heavens and the earth; indeed He Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful."

6.author: Arberry
6.text: Say: 'He sent it down, who knows the secret in the heavens and earth; He is All-forgiving, All-compassionate.'

6.author: Daryabadi
6.text: Say thou; He hath sent it down Who knoweth the secret of the heavens and the earth; verily He is ever Forgiving, Merciful.

6.author: Hilali & Khan
6.text: Say: "It (this Quran) has been sent down by Him (Allah) (the Real Lord of the heavens and earth) Who knows the secret of the heavens and the earth. Truly, He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

6.author: Itani
6.text: Say, “It was revealed by He who knows the Secret in the heavens and the earth. He is always Forgiving and Merciful.”

6.author: Maududi
6.text: O Muhammad, say to them, "This has been sent down by Him, Who knows the secret of the heavens and the earth." The fact is that He is very Forgiving and Merciful.

6.author: Mubarakpuri
6.text: Say: "It has been sent down by Him Who knows the secret of the heavens and the earth. Truly, He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

6.author: Pickthall
6.text: Say (unto them, O Muhammad): He who knoweth the secret of the heavens and the earth hath revealed it. Lo! He ever is Forgiving, Merciful.

6.author: Qarai
6.text: Say, ‘It has been sent down by Him who knows the hidden in the heavens and the earth. Indeed He is all-forgiving, all-merciful.’

6.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
6.text: Say: 'It was sent down by Him who knows the secrets of heavens and earth. He is Forgiving, the Most Merciful.

6.author: Saheeh International
6.text: Say, [O Muhammad], "It has been revealed by He who knows [every] secret within the heavens and the earth. Indeed, He is ever Forgiving and Merciful."

6.author: Sarwar
6.text: (Muhammad), tell them, "The One who knows all the secrets of the heavens and the earth has revealed it; He is All-forgiving and All-merciful."

6.author: Shakir
6.text: Say: He has revealed it Who knows the secret in the heavens and the earth; surely He is ever Forgiving, Merciful.

6.author: Wahiduddin Khan
6.text: Say to them, "It has been revealed by Him who knows every secret that is in the heavens and on the earth. Truly, He is most forgiving and most merciful."

6.author: Yusuf Ali
6.text: Say: "The (Qur'an) was sent down by Him who knows the mystery (that is) in the heavens and the earth: verily He is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful."

6.author: Transliteration
6.text: Qul anzalahu allathee yaAAlamu alssirra fee alssamawati waalardi innahu kana ghafooran raheeman

7.author: Ahmed Ali
7.text: But they say: "What sort of prophet is this who eats food and walks the market places? Why was no angel sent to him to act as admonisher with him?

7.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
7.text: And they said, “What is the matter with this (Noble) Messenger, that he eats food and walks in the markets? Why was not an angel sent down along with him, to give warning along with him?”

7.author: Arberry
7.text: They also say, 'What ails this Messenger that he eats food, and goes in the markets? Why has an angel not been sent down to him, to be a warner with him?

7.author: Daryabadi
7.text: And they say: what aileth this apostle: he eateth food and walketh about the market-places: wherefore is not an angel sent down unto him, so thathe may be along with him a warner '

7.author: Hilali & Khan
7.text: And they say: "Why does this Messenger (Muhammad SAW) eat food, and walk about in the markets (like ourselves). Why is not an angel sent down to him to be a warner with him?

7.author: Itani
7.text: And they say, “What sort of messenger is this, who eats food, and walks in the marketplaces? If only an angel was sent down with him, to be alongside him a warner.”

7.author: Maududi
7.text: They say, "What sort of a Messenger is he that he eats food and moves about in the streets? Why has not an angel been sent down to accompany him and threaten (the disbelievers)?

7.author: Mubarakpuri
7.text: And they say: "Why does this Messenger eat food, and walk about in the markets. Why is not an angel sent down to him to be a warner with him"

7.author: Pickthall
7.text: And they say: What aileth this messenger (of Allah) that he eateth food and walketh in the markets? Why is not an angel sent down unto him, to be a warner with him.

7.author: Qarai
7.text: And they say, ‘What sort of apostle is this who eats food and walks in the marketplaces? Why has not an angel been sent down to him so as to be a warner along with him?’

7.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
7.text: They also say: 'How is it that this Messenger eats food and walks about the markets? Why has no angel been sent down with him to warn us?

7.author: Saheeh International
7.text: And they say, "What is this messenger that eats food and walks in the markets? Why was there not sent down to him an angel so he would be with him a warner?

7.author: Sarwar
7.text: They say, "Why does this Messenger eat food, and walk in the streets? Why has not an angel been sent to him so that they could preach the message together?

7.author: Shakir
7.text: And they say: What is the matter with this Apostle that he eats food and goes about in the markets; why has not an angel been sent down to him, so that he should have been a warner with him?

7.author: Wahiduddin Khan
7.text: They say, "What kind of a messenger is this who eats food and walks about in the market-places? Why has no angel been sent down with him to warn us?"

7.author: Yusuf Ali
7.text: And they say: "What sort of a messenger is this, who eats food, and walks through the streets? Why has not an angel been sent down to him to give admonition with him?

7.author: Transliteration
7.text: Waqaloo mali hatha alrrasooli yakulu alttaAAama wayamshee fee alaswaqi lawla onzila ilayhi malakun fayakoona maAAahu natheeran

8.author: Ahmed Ali
8.text: Or a treasure should have been given to him, or he should have had an orchard from which he could eat." And these wicked people say: "You only follow a man ensorcelled."

8.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
8.text: “Or that he should have obtained a hidden treasure, or should have had a garden from which to eat”; and the unjust said, “You do not follow but a man under a magic spell.”

8.author: Arberry
8.text: Or why is not a treasure thrown to him, or why has he not a Garden to eat of?' The evildoers say, 'You are only following a man bewitched!'

8.author: Daryabadi
8.text: Or, whereforeis not there cast down unto him a treasure or he has a garden whereof he may eat! And the wrong-doeray: ye follow only a man bewitched.

8.author: Hilali & Khan
8.text: "Or (why) has not a treasure been granted to him, or why has he not a garden whereof he may eat?" And the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc.) say: "You follow none but a man bewitched."

8.author: Itani
8.text: Or, “If only a treasure was dropped on him.” Or, “If only he had a garden from which he eats.” The evildoers also say, “You are following but a man under spell.”

8.author: Maududi
8.text: Or why has not at least a treasure been sent down for him or a garden given to him for (easy) sustenance?" And the wicked people say, "You are following a man bewitched."

8.author: Mubarakpuri
8.text: "Or (why) has not a treasure been granted to him, or why has he not a garden whereof he may eat" And the wrongdoers say: "You follow none but a man bewitched."

8.author: Pickthall
8.text: Or (why is not) treasure thrown down unto him, or why hath he not a paradise from whence to eat? And the evil-doers say: Ye are but following a man bewitched.

8.author: Qarai
8.text: Or, ‘[Why is not] a treasure thrown to him, or [why does] he [not] have a garden from which he may eat?’ And the wrongdoers say, ‘You are just following a bewitched man.’

8.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
8.text: Or, why has no treasure been thrown to him, or a garden for him to eat from' And the harmdoers say: 'The man you follow is surely bewitched'

8.author: Saheeh International
8.text: Or [why is not] a treasure presented to him [from heaven], or does he [not] have a garden from which he eats?" And the wrongdoers say, "You follow not but a man affected by magic."

8.author: Sarwar
8.text: Why has a treasure not been laid out for him or a garden from which he could eat been given to him." The unjust ones say, "You are merely following a bewitched person".

8.author: Shakir
8.text: Or (why is not) a treasure sent down to him, or he is made to have a garden from which he should eat? And the unjust say: You do not follow any but a man deprived of reason.

8.author: Wahiduddin Khan
8.text: Or a treasure should have been sent down to him, or he should have had a garden from which to eat." The wrongdoers say, "You are surely following a man who is bewitched."

8.author: Yusuf Ali
8.text: "Or (Why) has not a treasure been bestowed on him, or why has he (not) a garden for enjoyment?" The wicked say: "Ye follow none other than a man bewitched."

8.author: Transliteration
8.text: Aw yulqa ilayhi kanzun aw takoonu lahu jannatun yakulu minha waqala alththalimoona in tattabiAAoona illa rajulan mashooran

9.author: Ahmed Ali
9.text: Just see what comparisons they bring up for you! They are lost and cannot find the way.

9.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
9.text: O dear Prophet (Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) see how they invent examples for you – they have therefore gone astray, unable find a road.

9.author: Arberry
9.text: Behold, how they strike similitudes for thee, and go astray, and are unable to find a way!

9.author: Daryabadi
9.text: Behold how they propound similitudes for thee! so they have strayed and cannot find a way.

9.author: Hilali & Khan
9.text: See how they coin similitudes for you, so they have gone astray, and they cannot find a (Right) Path.

9.author: Itani
9.text: Look how they invent examples for you. They have gone astray, and cannot find a way.

9.author: Maududi
9.text: Just see what strange arguments they bring forward with regard to you! They have gone so far astray that they cannot charge any thing against you.

9.author: Mubarakpuri
9.text: See how they coin similitudes for you, so they have gone astray, and they cannot find a path.

9.author: Pickthall
9.text: See how they coin similitudes for thee, so that they are all astray and cannot find a road!

9.author: Qarai
9.text: Look, how they coin epithets for you; so they go astray, and cannot find the way.

9.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
9.text: See how they strike examples for you, surely they have gone astray and are unable to find a way.

9.author: Saheeh International
9.text: Look how they strike for you comparisons; but they have strayed, so they cannot [find] a way.

9.author: Sarwar
9.text: Look at their various views about you! They have gone astray and are not able to find the right path.

9.author: Shakir
9.text: See what likenesses do they apply to you, so they have gone astray, therefore they shall not be able to find a way.

9.author: Wahiduddin Khan
9.text: Observe what kind of things they attribute to you. They have surely gone astray and cannot find the right way again.

9.author: Yusuf Ali
9.text: See what kinds of comparisons they make for thee! But they have gone astray, and never a way will they be able to find!

9.author: Transliteration
9.text: Onthur kayfa daraboo laka alamthala fadalloo fala yastateeAAoona sabeelan

10.author: Ahmed Ali
10.text: Blessed be He who, if He pleased, could give you better than that, -- gardens with rivers flowing by; and make palaces for you.

10.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
10.text: Most Auspicious is He Who, if He wills, may make it much better for you – Gardens beneath which rivers flow, and may create high palaces for you.

10.author: Arberry
10.text: Blessed be He who, if He will, shall assign to them better than that -- gardens underneath which rivers flow, and he shall assign to thee palaces.

10.author: Daryabadi
10.text: Blest is He Who, if He willed, shall appoint for them something better than that: gardens whereunder the rivers flow, and appoint for them palaces.

10.author: Hilali & Khan
10.text: Blessed be He Who, if He will, will assign you better than (all) that, - Gardens under which rivers flow (Paradise) and will assign you palaces (i.e. in Paradise).

10.author: Itani
10.text: Blessed is He who, if He wills, can provide you with better than that—gardens beneath which rivers flow—and He will give you palaces.

10.author: Maududi
10.text: Highly blessed is He, Who, if He wills, could give you much more and better things than what they propose for you: (Not one but) many gardens, beneath which rivers flow; and big castles:

10.author: Mubarakpuri
10.text: Blessed be He Who, if He wills, will assign you better than (all) that -- Gardens under which rivers flow and will assign you palaces.

10.author: Pickthall
10.text: Blessed is He Who, if He will, will assign thee better than (all) that - Gardens underneath which rivers flow - and will assign thee mansions.

10.author: Qarai
10.text: Blessed is He who will grant you better than that if He wishes—gardens with streams running in them, and He will make for you palaces.

10.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
10.text: Blessed be He who, if He wills, can assign you better things than these; gardens underneath which rivers flow, and He shall assign for you palaces.

10.author: Saheeh International
10.text: Blessed is He who, if He willed, could have made for you [something] better than that - gardens beneath which rivers flow - and could make for you palaces.

10.author: Sarwar
10.text: Blessed is He who could give you palaces and gardens wherein streams would flow, far better than what they want you to have.

10.author: Shakir
10.text: Blessed is He Who, if He please, will give you what is better than this, gardens beneath which rivers flow, and He will give you palaces.

10.author: Wahiduddin Khan
10.text: Blessed is He who, if He please, can give you better things than that; gardens watered by flowing streams, and palaces too.

10.author: Yusuf Ali
10.text: Blessed is He who, if that were His will, could give thee better (things) than those,- Gardens beneath which rivers flow; and He could give thee palaces (secure to dwell in).

10.author: Transliteration
10.text: Tabaraka allathee in shaa jaAAala laka khayran min thalika jannatin tajree min tahtiha alanharu wayajAAal laka qusooran

11.author: Ahmed Ali
11.text: Yet they reject the Hour as untrue. We have prepared a Fire for those who deny the Resurrection.

11.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
11.text: In fact they deny the Last Day; and We have kept prepared a blazing fire for those who deny the Last Day.

11.author: Arberry
11.text: Nay, but they cry lies to the Hour; and We have prepared for him who cries lies to the Hour a Blaze.

11.author: Daryabadi
11.text: Aye! they belie the Hour; and We have gotten ready for him who belieth the Hour a Flame.

11.author: Hilali & Khan
11.text: Nay, they deny the Hour (the Day of Resurrection), and for those who deny the Hour, We have prepared a flaming Fire (i.e. Hell).

11.author: Itani
11.text: In fact, they have denied the Hour, and We have prepared for those who deny the Hour a Blaze.

11.author: Maududi
11.text: The fact of the matter is that these people have denied "the Hour", --And for the one who denies the coming of the Hour, We have prepared a blazing Fire.

11.author: Mubarakpuri
11.text: Nay, they deny the Hour, and for those who deny the Hour, We have prepared a flaming Fire.

11.author: Pickthall
11.text: Nay, but they deny (the coming of) the Hour, and for those who deny (the coming of) the Hour We have prepared a flame.

11.author: Qarai
11.text: Indeed, they deny the Hour, and We have prepared a Blaze for those who deny the Hour.

11.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
11.text: No, they belied the Hour. We have prepared for him who belied the Hour a Blaze.

11.author: Saheeh International
11.text: But they have denied the Hour, and We have prepared for those who deny the Hour a Blaze.

11.author: Sarwar
11.text: They deny the Hour of Doom so We have prepared for them a burning fire.

11.author: Shakir
11.text: But they reject the hour, and We have prepared a burning fire for him who rejects the hour.

11.author: Wahiduddin Khan
11.text: They deny the Hour. For those who deny that Hour, We have prepared a blazing fire.

11.author: Yusuf Ali
11.text: Nay they deny the hour (of the judgment to come): but We have prepared a blazing fire for such as deny the hour:

11.author: Transliteration
11.text: Bal kaththaboo bialssaAAati waaAAtadna liman kaththaba bialssaAAati saAAeeran

12.author: Ahmed Ali
12.text: When (Hell) appears to them from a distance they will hear it raging and roaring.

12.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
12.text: When it will see them from afar, they will hear its raging and roaring.

12.author: Arberry
12.text: When it sees them from a far place, they shall hear its bubbling and sighing.

12.author: Daryabadi
12.text: When it beholdeth them from afar, they shall hear it raging and roaring.

12.author: Hilali & Khan
12.text: When it (Hell) sees them from a far place, they will hear its raging and its roaring.

12.author: Itani
12.text: When it sees them from a distant place, they will hear it raging and roaring.

12.author: Maududi
12.text: When it will see them from afar, they will hear the sounds of its raging and roaring.

12.author: Mubarakpuri
12.text: When it sees them from a far place, they will hear its raging and its roaring.

12.author: Pickthall
12.text: When it seeth them from afar, they hear the crackling and the roar thereof.

12.author: Qarai
12.text: When it sights them from a distant place, they will hear it raging and roaring.

12.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
12.text: When it sees them from a far off place, they shall hear it raging and sighing.

12.author: Saheeh International
12.text: When the Hellfire sees them from a distant place, they will hear its fury and roaring.

12.author: Sarwar
12.text: Even if they were to see [this fire] from a distant place, they would only listen to its raging and roaring.

12.author: Shakir
12.text: When it shall come into their sight from a distant place, they shall hear its vehement raging and roaring.

12.author: Wahiduddin Khan
12.text: When it sees them from afar, they will hear its raging and roaring.

12.author: Yusuf Ali
12.text: When it sees them from a place far off, they will hear its fury and its ranging sigh.

12.author: Transliteration
12.text: Itha raathum min makanin baAAeedin samiAAoo laha taghayyuthan wazafeeran

13.author: Ahmed Ali
13.text: And when they are cast within a narrow space of it chained together, they would plead for death.

13.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
13.text: And when they are thrown into a narrow place in it, bound in chains, they will pray for death in it.

13.author: Arberry
13.text: And when they are cast, coupled in fetters, into a narrow place of that Fire, they will call out there for destruction.

13.author: Daryabadi
13.text: And when they are flung into a strait place thereof, bound Up, they shall call therein unto death.

13.author: Hilali & Khan
13.text: And when they shall be thrown into a narrow place thereof, chained together, they will exclaim therein for destruction.

13.author: Itani
13.text: And when they are thrown into it, into a tight place, shackled, they will plead there for death.

13.author: Maududi
13.text: And when they are chained together and flung into a narrow space therein, they will begin to call for death.

13.author: Mubarakpuri
13.text: And when they shall be thrown into a narrow place thereof, chained together, they will exclaim therein for destruction.

13.author: Pickthall
13.text: And when they are flung into a narrow place thereof, chained together, they pray for destruction there.

13.author: Qarai
13.text: And when they are cast into a narrow place in it, bound together [in chains], they will pray for [their own] annihilation.

13.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
13.text: And when, chained in (iron) fetters, they are cast into some narrow space of the Fire, they will call out for destruction.

13.author: Saheeh International
13.text: And when they are thrown into a narrow place therein bound in chains, they will cry out thereupon for destruction.

13.author: Sarwar
13.text: When they are thrown, bound, into a narrow place therein, then only will they wish for their death.

13.author: Shakir
13.text: And when they are cast into a narrow place in it, bound, they shall there call out for destruction.

13.author: Wahiduddin Khan
13.text: When they are thrown into a narrow space, chained together, they will plead for death.

13.author: Yusuf Ali
13.text: And when they are cast, bound together into a constricted place therein, they will plead for destruction there and then!

13.author: Transliteration
13.text: Waitha olqoo minha makanan dayyiqan muqarraneena daAAaw hunalika thubooran

14.author: Ahmed Ali
14.text: "Do not ask for one death but many deaths on this day."

14.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
14.text: It will be said “Do not pray this day for one death – and pray for many deaths.”

14.author: Arberry
14.text: 'Call not out today for one destruction, but call for many!'

14.author: Daryabadi
14.text: Call not to-day for a single death but call for death manifold.

14.author: Hilali & Khan
14.text: Exclaim not today for one destruction, but exclaim for many destructions.

14.author: Itani
14.text: “Do not plead for one death today, but plead for a great many deaths.”

14.author: Maududi
14.text: (Then it will be said to them:) "Do not call for one death today, but call for many deaths."

14.author: Mubarakpuri
14.text: Exclaim not today for one destruction, but exclaim for many destructions.

14.author: Pickthall
14.text: Pray not that day for one destruction, but pray for many destructions!

14.author: Qarai
14.text: [They will be told:] ‘Do not pray for a single annihilation today, but pray for many annihilations!’

14.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
14.text: 'Do not call out today for one destruction; call out for many destructions'

14.author: Saheeh International
14.text: [They will be told], "Do not cry this Day for one destruction but cry for much destruction."

14.author: Sarwar
14.text: They will be told, "Do not pray to die only once but pray to die many times."

14.author: Shakir
14.text: Call not this day for one destruction, but call for destructions many.

14.author: Wahiduddin Khan
14.text: But they will be told, "Do not call today for one death, call for many deaths!"

14.author: Yusuf Ali
14.text: "This day plead not for a single destruction: plead for destruction oft-repeated!"

14.author: Transliteration
14.text: La tadAAoo alyawma thubooran wahidan waodAAoo thubooran katheeran

15.author: Ahmed Ali
15.text: Ask them: "Is this better or a garden for everlasting abode which has been promised the pious and devout? It would be their guerdon and their destination.

15.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
15.text: Say, “Is this better or the Everlasting Gardens which are promised to those who fear? That is their reward and their outcome.”

15.author: Arberry
15.text: Say: 'Is that better, or the Garden of Eternity, that is promised to the godfearing, and is their recompense and homecoming?'

15.author: Daryabadi
15.text: Say thou: in that better or Garden of Abidence that hath been promised to the God-fearing! It shall be theirs as a recompense and a retreat.

15.author: Hilali & Khan
15.text: Say: (O Muhammad SAW) "Is that (torment) better or the Paradise of Eternity promised to the Muttaqun (pious and righteous persons - see V. 2:2)?" It will be theirs as a reward and as a final destination.

15.author: Itani
15.text: Say, “Is this better, or the Garden of Eternity promised to the righteous? It is for them a reward and a destination.

15.author: Maududi
15.text: Ask them "Is this (Fire) better or the everlasting Garden which has been promised to the God-fearing righteous people?" which will be the recompense of their good deeds and the final destination of their journey

15.author: Mubarakpuri
15.text: Say: "Is that better or the Paradise of Eternity which is promised to those who have Taqwa" It will be theirs as a reward and final destination.

15.author: Pickthall
15.text: Say: Is that (doom) better or the Garden of Immortality which is promised unto those who ward off (evil)? It will be their reward and journey's end.

15.author: Qarai
15.text: Say, ‘Is that better, or the everlasting paradise promised to the Godwary, which will be their reward and destination?’

15.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
15.text: Say: 'Is that better, or the Garden of Eternity which the cautious have been promised? It is their recompense and their arrival'

15.author: Saheeh International
15.text: Say, "Is that better or the Garden of Eternity which is promised to the righteous? It will be for them a reward and destination.

15.author: Sarwar
15.text: (Muhammad), ask them, "Is what you want better or the eternal garden promised to the pious ones as their reward and dwelling?

15.author: Shakir
15.text: Say: Is this better or the abiding garden which those who guard (against evil) are promised? That shall be a reward and a resort for them.

15.author: Wahiduddin Khan
15.text: Say, "Which is better, this or the Paradise of immortality which the righteous have been promised? It is their recompense and their destination."

15.author: Yusuf Ali
15.text: Say: "Is that best, or the eternal garden, promised to the righteous? for them, that is a reward as well as a goal (of attainment).

15.author: Transliteration
15.text: Qul athalika khayrun am jannatu alkhuldi allatee wuAAida almuttaqoona kanat lahum jazaan wamaseeran

16.author: Ahmed Ali
16.text: There will they have whatever they wish, and there abide for ever." This is a promise incumbent on your Lord which will certainly be fulfilled.

16.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
16.text: “In it for them is all that they may desire, abiding in it for ever; a promise incumbent upon your Lord, that is prayed for.”

16.author: Arberry
16.text: Therein they shall have what they will dwelling forever; it is a promise binding upon thy Lord, and of Him to be required.

16.author: Daryabadi
16.text: Theirs therein shall be all that they wish for, as abiders; a promise from thy Lord to be asked for.

16.author: Hilali & Khan
16.text: For them there will be therein all that they desire, and they will abide (there forever). It is a promise binding upon your Lord that must be fulfilled.

16.author: Itani
16.text: They will have therein whatever they desire, forever. That is upon your Lord a binding promise.

16.author: Maududi
16.text: wherein they will get everything they desire and wherein they will dwell for ever. This is a promise which your Lord has taken upon Himself to fulfil.

16.author: Mubarakpuri
16.text: For them there will be therein all that they desire, and they will abide (therein forever). It is upon your Lord a Wa`dan Mas'ula.

16.author: Pickthall
16.text: Therein abiding, they have all that they desire. It is for thy Lord a promise that must be fulfilled.

16.author: Qarai
16.text: There they will have whatever they wish, abiding [forever]—a promise [much] besought, [binding] on your Lord.

16.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
16.text: Living there for ever, they shall find in it all that they desire. That is a promise binding upon your Lord, and to be asked of Him.

16.author: Saheeh International
16.text: For them therein is whatever they wish, [while] abiding eternally. It is ever upon your Lord a promise [worthy to be] requested.

16.author: Sarwar
16.text: Therein they will eternally have whatever they want. This is a binding promise from your Lord."

16.author: Shakir
16.text: They shall have therein what they desire abiding (in it); it is a promise which it is proper to be prayed for from your Lord.

16.author: Wahiduddin Khan
16.text: Abiding there forever, they shall find in it all that they desire. This is a binding promise which your Lord has made.

16.author: Yusuf Ali
16.text: "For them there will be therein all that they wish for: they will dwell (there) for aye: A promise to be prayed for from thy Lord."

16.author: Transliteration
16.text: Lahum feeha ma yashaoona khalideena kana AAala rabbika waAAdan masoolan

17.author: Ahmed Ali
17.text: The day He will gather them together along with those they worshipped other than God, He will ask them: "Did you lure these creatures of Mine away, or did they themselves go astray?"

17.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
17.text: And on the day when He will assemble them and all what they worship instead of Allah – then will say to those deities, “Did you mislead my bondmen or did they themselves forget the path?”

17.author: Arberry
17.text: Upon the day when He shall muster them and that they serve, apart from God, and He shall say, 'Was it you that led these My servants astray, or did they themselves err from the way?'

17.author: Daryabadi
17.text: And on the Dav whereon He will gather them and that v; hich they worship beside Allah and will say. are ye the ones who sent astray these My bondmen; or strayed they themselves from the way!

17.author: Hilali & Khan
17.text: And on the Day when He will gather them together and that which they worship besides Allah [idols, angels, pious men, saints, 'Iesa (Jesus) - son of Maryam (Mary), etc.]. He will say: "Was it you who misled these My slaves or did they (themselves) stray from the (Right) Path?"

17.author: Itani
17.text: On the Day when He gathers them, and what they worshiped besides God, He will say, “Was it you who misled these servants of Mine, or was it they who lost the way?”

17.author: Maududi
17.text: And on that Day (your Lord will gather these people together as well as their deities, whom they worship besides AIlah. Then He will ask them, "Did you mislead these servants of Mine, or did they themselves go astray?"

17.author: Mubarakpuri
17.text: And on the Day when He will gather them together and that which they worship besides Allah. He will say: "Was it you who misled these My servants or did they stray from the path"

17.author: Pickthall
17.text: And on the day when He will assemble them and that which they worship instead of Allah and will say: Was it ye who misled these my slaves or did they (themselves) wander from the way?

17.author: Qarai
17.text: On the day that He will muster them and those whom they worship besides Allah, He will say, ‘Was it you who led astray these servants of Mine, or did they themselves stray from the way?’

17.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
17.text: On the Day when He gathers them with all that they worship, other than Allah, He will say: 'Was it you who misled My worshipers, or did they themselves go astray'

17.author: Saheeh International
17.text: And [mention] the Day He will gather them and that which they worship besides Allah and will say, "Did you mislead these, My servants, or did they [themselves] stray from the way?"

17.author: Sarwar
17.text: On the day when the unbelievers and whatever they had been worshipping besides God will be resurrected, He will ask the idols, "Did you mislead My servants or did they themselves go astray from the right path?"

17.author: Shakir
17.text: And on the day when He shall gather them, and whatever they served besides Allah, He shall say: Was it you who led astray these My servants, or did they themselves go astray from the path?

17.author: Wahiduddin Khan
17.text: On the Day He gathers them all together with those they worship besides Him, He will say, "Was it you who misled My servants, or did they stray away by themselves?"

17.author: Yusuf Ali
17.text: The day He will gather them together as well as those whom they worship besides Allah, He will ask: "Was it ye who let these My servants astray, or did they stray from the Path themselves?"

17.author: Transliteration
17.text: Wayawma yahshuruhum wama yaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi fayaqoolu aantum adlaltum AAibadee haolai am hum dalloo alssabeela

18.author: Ahmed Ali
18.text: They will answer: "Glory to You. It was not worthy of us to seek any protector other than You. But You allowed them and their fathers a life of ease until they turned oblivious of the Reminder. They were a people impenitent.

18.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
18.text: They will submit, “Purity is to You – it was not rightful for us to appoint any Master besides You; but You gave them and their forefathers usage to the extent that they forgot Your Remembrance; and they were indeed the people who destroy themselves.”

18.author: Arberry
18.text: They shall 'Glory be to Thee! It did not behove us to take unto say, ourselves protectors apart from Thee; but Thou gavest them and their fathers enjoyment of days, until they forgot

18.author: Daryabadi
18.text: They will say; hallowed be Thou! it behoved us not that we should take beside Thee any patron, but Thou allovvedest them and their fathers enjoyment Until they forgat the admonition, and they were a people doomed.

18.author: Hilali & Khan
18.text: They will say: "Glorified be You! It was not for us to take any Auliya' (Protectors, Helpers, etc.) besides You, but You gave them and their fathers comfort till they forgot the warning, and became a lost people (doomed to total loss).

18.author: Itani
18.text: They will say, “Glory be to You. It was not for us to take any lords besides You. But you gave them enjoyments, and their ancestors, until they forgot the Message, and became ruined people.”

18.author: Maududi
18.text: They will answer, "Glory be to Thee! We dared not take anyguardian besides Thee: (they were misled because) Thou didst give them and their forefathers all the good things of life till they forgot the Admonition, and incurred the punishment. "

18.author: Mubarakpuri
18.text: They will say: "Glorified be You! It was not for us to take any Awliya' besides You, but You gave them and their fathers comfort till they forgot the warning, and became a lost people."

18.author: Pickthall
18.text: They will say: Be Thou Glorified! it was not for us to choose any protecting friends beside thee; but Thou didst give them and their fathers ease till they forgot the warning and became lost folk.

18.author: Qarai
18.text: They will say, ‘Immaculate are You! It does not behoove us to take any wali in Your stead! But You provided for them and their fathers until they forgot the Reminder, and they were a ruined lot.’

18.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
18.text: They will answer: 'Exaltations to You. We should not have taken others for a guardian, but You gave them and their fathers enjoyment until they forgot Your Remembrance and they were a destroyed nation'

18.author: Saheeh International
18.text: They will say, "Exalted are You! It was not for us to take besides You any allies. But You provided comforts for them and their fathers until they forgot the message and became a people ruined."

18.author: Sarwar
18.text: They will reply, "Lord, You alone deserve all glory! We were not supposed to choose any guardian other than you. Since you have been benevolent to these people and their fathers, they forgot Your guidance and, thus, became subject to perdition."

18.author: Shakir
18.text: They shall say: Glory be to Thee; it was not beseeming for us that we should take any guardians besides Thee, but Thou didst make them and their fathers to enjoy until they forsook the reminder, and they were a people in perdition,

18.author: Wahiduddin Khan
18.text: They will answer, "Hallowed be You! It was not proper for us to choose any guardian other than You. But You gave them and their fathers the comforts of this life, so that they forgot Your reminder and thus brought destruction upon themselves."

18.author: Yusuf Ali
18.text: They will say: "Glory to Thee! not meet was it for us that we should take for protectors others besides Thee: But Thou didst bestow, on them and their fathers, good things (in life), until they forgot the Message: for they were a people (worthless and) lost."

18.author: Transliteration
18.text: Qaloo subhanaka ma kana yanbaghee lana an nattakhitha min doonika min awliyaa walakin mattaAAtahum waabaahum hatta nasoo alththikra wakanoo qawman booran

19.author: Ahmed Ali
19.text: (The idolaters will be told): "Your gods have refuted your assertion. You can neither avert (your doom) nor receive any help. We shall make the wicked among you taste of severe punishment."

19.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
19.text: “So the deities have belied what you state – so now you can neither avert the punishment nor help yourself; and whoever among you is unjust, We shall make him taste a terrible punishment.”

19.author: Arberry
19.text: the Remembrance, and were a people corrupt.' So they cried lies touching the things you say, and you can neither turn it aside, nor find any help. Whosoever of you does evil, We shall let him taste a great chastisement.

19.author: Daryabadi
19.text: So now they belie you in that which ye said; so ye are not able to obtain diversion nor help. And who soever of you doth wrong, him We shall make taste a great torment.

19.author: Hilali & Khan
19.text: Thus they (false gods all deities other than Allah) will give you (polytheists) the lie regarding what you say (that they are gods besides Allah), then you can neither avert (the punishment), nor get help. And whoever among you does wrong (i.e. sets up rivals to Allah), We shall make him taste a great torment.

19.author: Itani
19.text: They have denied you because of what you say; so you can neither avert, nor help. Whoever among you commits injustice, We will make him taste a grievous punishment.

19.author: Maududi
19.text: Thus will your gods deny alI that you are professing today. Then you shall neither be able to repel your punishment nor shall get any help from anywhere; and whoso is guilty. of iniquity'' among you, We shall make him taste a severe torment.

19.author: Mubarakpuri
19.text: Thus they will deny you in what you say; then you can neither avert nor find help. And whoever among you does wrong, We shall make him taste a great torment.

19.author: Pickthall
19.text: Thus they will give you the lie regarding what ye say, then ye can neither avert (the doom) nor obtain help. And whoso among you doeth wrong, We shall make him taste great torment.

19.author: Qarai
19.text: So they will certainly impugn you in what you say, and you will neither be able to circumvent [punishment] nor find help, and whoever of you does wrong, We shall make him taste a terrible punishment.

19.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
19.text: So they belie what you say, and you can neither turn it aside, nor find any help. Those of you who have done evil, We let them taste a great punishment.

19.author: Saheeh International
19.text: So they will deny you, [disbelievers], in what you say, and you cannot avert [punishment] or [find] help. And whoever commits injustice among you - We will make him taste a great punishment.

19.author: Sarwar
19.text: God will say (to the idolaters), "Your idols have rejected your faith. You cannot avoid their rejection nor can you find any help. Anyone of you who commits injustice will be made to suffer a great torment."

19.author: Shakir
19.text: So they shall indeed give you the lie in what you say, then you shall not be able to ward off or help, and whoever among you is unjust, We will make him taste a great chastisement.

19.author: Wahiduddin Khan
19.text: [God will say], "Now, they have given the lie to all your assertions, and you can neither ward off [your punishment] nor obtain any help." For, whoever of you has committed evil, shall be caused by Us to taste great suffering!"

19.author: Yusuf Ali
19.text: (Allah will say): "Now have they proved you liars in what ye say: so ye cannot avert (your penalty) nor (get) help." And whoever among you does wrong, him shall We cause to taste of a grievous Penalty.

19.author: Transliteration
19.text: Faqad kaththabookum bima taqooloona fama tastateeAAoona sarfan wala nasran waman yathlim minkum nuthiqhu AAathaban kabeeran

20.author: Ahmed Ali
20.text: We never sent before you apostles who did not eat food and walk the market places. We make some of you the means of trying the others. So will you persevere? Your Lord is always watching.

20.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
20.text: And all the Noble Messengers We sent before you were like this – eating food and walking in the markets; and We have made some of you a test for others; so will you, O people, patiently endure? And O dear Prophet, your Lord is All Seeing.

20.author: Arberry
20.text: And We sent not before thee any Envoys, but that they ate food, and went in the markets; and We appointed some of you to be a trial for others: 'Will you endure?' Thy Lord is ever All-seeing.

20.author: Daryabadi
20.text: And We have not sent before thee ony of the sent ones but verily they ate food and walked about in the market places. And We have made some of you unto some others a temptation; will ye have patience! And thy Lord is ever a Beholder. pART XIX

20.author: Hilali & Khan
20.text: And We never sent before you (O Muhammad SAW) any of the Messengers but verily, they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you as a trial for others: will you have patience? And your Lord is Ever All-Seer (of everything).

20.author: Itani
20.text: We never sent any messengers before you, but they ate food and walked in the marketplaces. And We made some of you tempters for one another—will you be patient? Your Lord is always Observing.

20.author: Maududi
20.text: O Muhammad,, all the Messengers whom We sent before you also ate food and moved about in the streets. In fact, We have made you all a means of test for one another. Will you show patience? for your Lord sees everything.

20.author: Mubarakpuri
20.text: And We never sent before you any of the Messengers but verily, they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you as a trial for others; will you have patience And your Lord is Ever All-Seer.

20.author: Pickthall
20.text: We never sent before thee any messengers but lo! they verily ate food and walked in the markets. And We have appointed some of you a test for others: Will ye be steadfast? And thy Lord is ever Seer.

20.author: Qarai
20.text: We did not send any apostles before you but that they indeed ate food and walked in marketplaces. We have made you a [means of] test for one another, [to see] if you will be patient and steadfast, and your Lord is all-seeing.

20.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
20.text: We did not send Messengers before you but that they ate food and walked about in the markets, We have appointed some of you to be a trial for others. Will you endure? Your Lord is the Seer.

20.author: Saheeh International
20.text: And We did not send before you, [O Muhammad], any of the messengers except that they ate food and walked in the markets. And We have made some of you [people] as trial for others - will you have patience? And ever is your Lord, Seeing.

20.author: Sarwar
20.text: All the Messengerss whom We sent before, certainly, ate food and walked through the streets. We have made some of you (people) a trial for the others. Would you then exercise patience? Your Lord is All-aware.

20.author: Shakir
20.text: And We have not sent before you any apostles but they most surely ate food and went about in the markets; and We have made some of you a trial for others; will you bear patiently? And your Lord is ever Seeing.

20.author: Wahiduddin Khan
20.text: We never sent any messengers before you who did not eat food and walk in the market-place. We make some of you a means of trial for others, to see whether you are steadfast. Your Lord is all seeing.

20.author: Yusuf Ali
20.text: And the messengers whom We sent before thee were all (men) who ate food and walked through the streets: We have made some of you as a trial for others: will ye have patience? for Allah is One Who sees (all things).

20.author: Transliteration
20.text: Wama arsalna qablaka mina almursaleena illa innahum layakuloona alttaAAama wayamshoona fee alaswaqi wajaAAalna baAAdakum libaAAdin fitnatan atasbiroona wakana rabbuka baseeran

21.author: Ahmed Ali
21.text: Those who do not hope to meet Us say: "Why are no angels sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?" They are full of self-conceit and behave with intense arrogance.

21.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
21.text: And those who do not expect to meet Us said, “Why were the angels not sent down to us or we been able to see our Lord?” Indeed they thought too highly of themselves and they turned extremely rebellious.

21.author: Arberry
21.text: Say those who look not to encounter Us, 'Why have the angels not been sent down on us, or why see we not our Lord?' Waxed proud they have within them, and become greatly disdainful.

21.author: Daryabadi
21.text: And those who fear not the meeting with Usl say: wherefore are not angels sent down unto us or wherefore we see not our Lord! Assuredly they have proven stiff-necked in their souls and have exceeded the bounds with excess great.

21.author: Hilali & Khan
21.text: And those who expect not for a Meeting with Us (i.e. those who deny the Day of Resurrection and the life of the Hereafter), say: "Why are not the angels sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?" Indeed they think too highly of themselves, and are scornful with great pride.

21.author: Itani
21.text: Those who do not expect to meet Us say, “If only the angels were sent down to us, or we could see our Lord.” They have grown arrogant within themselves, and have become excessively defiant.

21.author: Maududi
21.text: Those people, who have no fear of coming before Us, say, "Why should not the angels be sent down to us? Or else we should see our Lord. " Great arrogance have they assumed in regard to themselves, and have transgressed all limits in their rebellion.

21.author: Mubarakpuri
21.text: And those who expect not a meeting with Us said: "Why are not the angels sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord" Indeed they think too highly of themselves, and are scornful with great pride.

21.author: Pickthall
21.text: And those who look not for a meeting with Us say: Why are angels not sent down unto us and (Why) do we not see our Lord! Assuredly they think too highly of themselves and are scornful with great pride.

21.author: Qarai
21.text: Those who do not expect to encounter Us say, ‘Why have angels not been sent down to us, or why do we not see our Lord?’ Certainly, they are full of arrogance within their souls and have become terribly defiant.

21.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
21.text: Those who do not hope to meet Us ask: 'Why have no angels been sent to us? Why can we not see our Lord' How proud they are within themselves, and have become greatly disdainful.

21.author: Saheeh International
21.text: And those who do not expect the meeting with Us say, "Why were not angels sent down to us, or [why] do we [not] see our Lord?" They have certainly become arrogant within themselves and [become] insolent with great insolence.

21.author: Sarwar
21.text: Those who have no desire to meet Us have said, "Would that the angels had been sent to us or that we could see our Lord." They are really filled with pride and have committed the greatest and worst kind of rebellion and hostility.

21.author: Shakir
21.text: And those who do not hope for Our meeting, say: Why have not angels been sent down upon us, or (why) do we not see our Lord? Now certainly they are too proud of themselves and have revolted in great revolt.

21.author: Wahiduddin Khan
21.text: Those who do not expect a meeting with Us say, "Why are angels not sent down to us?" Or "Why do we not see our Lord?" Surely, they are too proud of themselves and have greatly exceeded all bounds.

21.author: Yusuf Ali
21.text: Such as fear not the meeting with Us (for Judgment) say: "Why are not the angels sent down to us, or (why) do we not see our Lord?" Indeed they have an arrogant conceit of themselves, and mighty is the insolence of their impiety!

21.author: Transliteration
21.text: Waqala allatheena la yarjoona liqaana lawla onzila AAalayna almalaikatu aw nara rabbana laqadi istakbaroo fee anfusihim waAAataw AAutuwwan kabeeran

22.author: Ahmed Ali
22.text: The day they see the angels there will be no happy tidings for the sinners; and they will say: "There is an insurmountable barrier!"

22.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
22.text: The day when they will see the angels – that day will not be of any happiness for the guilty – and they will cry, “O God, erect a barrier between us and them!”

22.author: Arberry
22.text: Upon the day that they see the angels, no good tidings that day for the sinners; they shall say, 'A ban forbidden!'

22.author: Daryabadi
22.text: The Day whereon they shall behold the angels on that day there will be nea joy for the culprits, and they will say: away! away!

22.author: Hilali & Khan
22.text: On the Day they will see the angels, no glad tidings will there be for the Mujrimun (criminals, disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, etc.) that day. And they (angels) will say: "All kinds of glad tidings are forbidden for you," [None will be allowed to enter Paradise except the one who said: La ilaha ill-Allah, "(none has the right to be worshipped but Allah) and acted practically on its legal orders and obligations].

22.author: Itani
22.text: On the Day when they see the angels—there will be no good news for sinners on that Day; and they will say, “A protective refuge.”

22.author: Maududi
22.text: The Day, when they will see the angels, will not be a day of rejoicing for the criminals; they will cry out, "May Allah save us!"

22.author: Mubarakpuri
22.text: On the Day they will see the angels -- no good news will there be for the criminals that day. And they will say: "Hijran Mahjura."

22.author: Pickthall
22.text: On the day when they behold the angels, on that day there will be no good tidings for the guilty; and they will cry: A forbidding ban!

22.author: Qarai
22.text: The day they will see the angels, there will be no good news for the guilty on that day, and they will say, ‘Keep off [from paradise]!’

22.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
22.text: On the Day when they behold the angels, there will be no glad tidings for the sinners. They will say: 'A refuge which is forbidden'

22.author: Saheeh International
22.text: The day they see the angels - no good tidings will there be that day for the criminals, and [the angels] will say, "Prevented and inaccessible."

22.author: Sarwar
22.text: On the day when the criminals see the angels, there will, certainly, be no rejoicing for them. Rather, they will plead to the angels, "Please keep away from us (do not drive us into hell)."

22.author: Shakir
22.text: On the day when they shall see the angels, there shall be no joy on that day for the guilty, and they shall say: It is a forbidden thing totally prohibited.

22.author: Wahiduddin Khan
22.text: There will be no good tidings for the guilty on the day they see the angels; and they will cry out, "Keep away, keep away!"

22.author: Yusuf Ali
22.text: The Day they see the angels,- no joy will there be to the sinners that Day: The (angels) will say: "There is a barrier forbidden (to you) altogether!"

22.author: Transliteration
22.text: Yawma yarawna almalaikata la bushra yawmaithin lilmujrimeena wayaqooloona hijran mahjooran

23.author: Ahmed Ali
23.text: We shall turn to their deeds and scatter them like particles of dust.

23.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
23.text: And We turned all the deeds they had performed into scattered floating specks of dust.

23.author: Arberry
23.text: We shall advance upon what work they have done, and make it a scattered dust.

23.author: Daryabadi
23.text: And We shall set upon that which they worked, and shall make it as dust wind-scattered.

23.author: Hilali & Khan
23.text: And We shall turn to whatever deeds they (disbelievers, polytheists, sinners, etc.) did, and We shall make such deeds as scattered floating particles of dust.

23.author: Itani
23.text: We will proceed to the works they did, and will turn them into scattered dust.

23.author: Maududi
23.text: Then We shall turn to what they had done and render it vain like scattered dust.

23.author: Mubarakpuri
23.text: And We shall turn to whatever deeds they did, and We shall make such deeds as scattered floating particles of dust.

23.author: Pickthall
23.text: And We shall turn unto the work they did and make it scattered motes.

23.author: Qarai
23.text: Then We shall attend to the works they have done and then turn them into scattered dust.

23.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
23.text: Then We shall advance upon the work which they have done and render it as scattered dust.

23.author: Saheeh International
23.text: And We will regard what they have done of deeds and make them as dust dispersed.

23.author: Sarwar
23.text: We shall call their deeds into Our presence and scatter them into the air as dust (turn them devoid of all virtue).

23.author: Shakir
23.text: And We will proceed to what they have done of deeds, so We shall render them as scattered floating dust.

23.author: Wahiduddin Khan
23.text: and We shall take all that they did and turn it into scattered dust.

23.author: Yusuf Ali
23.text: And We shall turn to whatever deeds they did (in this life), and We shall make such deeds as floating dust scattered about.

23.author: Transliteration
23.text: Waqadimna ila ma AAamiloo min AAamalin fajaAAalnahu habaan manthooran

24.author: Ahmed Ali
24.text: The inmates of Paradise will have a better abode that day, and a better resting place.

24.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
24.text: And for the People of the Garden on that day is a better destination and account, and a better place of afternoon rest.

24.author: Arberry
24.text: The inhabitants of Paradise that day, better shall be their lodging, fairer their resting-place.

24.author: Daryabadi
24.text: Fellows of the Garden shall be on that Day in a goodly abode and a gocdly re pose.

24.author: Hilali & Khan
24.text: The dwellers of Paradise (i.e. those who deserved it through their Faith and righteousness) will, on that Day, have the best abode, and have the fairest of places for repose.

24.author: Itani
24.text: The companions of Paradise on that Day will be better lodged, and more fairly accommodated.

24.author: Maududi
24.text: (On the contrary) only those who have deserved the Garden, will have a good abode on that Day and a cool place for midday rest.

24.author: Mubarakpuri
24.text: The dwellers of Paradise will, on that Day, have the best abode, and have the fairest of places for repose.

24.author: Pickthall
24.text: Those who have earned the Garden on that day will be better in their home and happier in their place of noonday rest;

24.author: Qarai
24.text: On that day the inhabitants of paradise will be in the best abode and an excellent resting place.

24.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
24.text: On that Day, the companions of Paradise (will have) a better abode and a finer restingplace.

24.author: Saheeh International
24.text: The companions of Paradise, that Day, are [in] a better settlement and better resting place.

24.author: Sarwar
24.text: The dwellers of Paradise on that day will have the best residence and resting place.

24.author: Shakir
24.text: The dwellers of the garden shall on that day be in a better abiding-place and a better resting-place.

24.author: Wahiduddin Khan
24.text: The inhabitants of the Garden will have the best residence and the finest lodging on that Day.

24.author: Yusuf Ali
24.text: The Companions of the Garden will be well, that Day, in their abode, and have the fairest of places for repose.

24.author: Transliteration
24.text: Ashabu aljannati yawmaithin khayrun mustaqarran waahsanu maqeelan

25.author: Ahmed Ali
25.text: The day the heavens splits asunder with a dazzling white cloud gathering and the angels descend in a continuous stream,

25.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
25.text: And the day when the sky will be split asunder with clouds and the angels will be sent down in full.

25.author: Arberry
25.text: Upon the day that heaven is split asunder with the clouds and the angels are sent down in majesty,

25.author: Daryabadi
25.text: And on the Day whereon the heaven shall be rent asunderg from the q clouds and the angelhall be sent downll with a great descending.

25.author: Hilali & Khan
25.text: And (remember) the Day when the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and the angels will be sent down, with a grand descending.

25.author: Itani
25.text: The Day when the sky is cleft with clouds, and the angels are sent down in streams.

25.author: Maududi
25.text: On that Day, a cloud will appear rending the sky and the angels will be senthi down rank after rank.

25.author: Mubarakpuri
25.text: And (remember) the Day when the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and the angels will be sent down, with a grand descending.

25.author: Pickthall
25.text: A day when the heaven with the clouds will be rent asunder and the angels will be sent down, a grand descent.

25.author: Qarai
25.text: The day when the sky with its clouds will be split open, and the angels will be sent down [in a majestic] descent,

25.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
25.text: On that Day, the heaven is split asunder with clouds and the angels are sent down in majesty,

25.author: Saheeh International
25.text: And [mention] the Day when the heaven will split open with [emerging] clouds, and the angels will be sent down in successive descent.

25.author: Sarwar
25.text: On that day the sky will be crystal blue, clear of clouds. The angels will descend in groups

25.author: Shakir
25.text: And on the day when the heaven shall burst asunder with the clouds, and the angels shall be sent down descending (in ranks).

25.author: Wahiduddin Khan
25.text: On a Day when the sky will split open with its clouds and the angels are sent down rank upon rank,

25.author: Yusuf Ali
25.text: The Day the heaven shall be rent asunder with clouds, and angels shall be sent down, descending (in ranks),-

25.author: Transliteration
25.text: Wayawma tashaqqaqu alssamao bialghamami wanuzzila almalaikatu tanzeelan

26.author: Ahmed Ali
26.text: The real sovereignty will belong to Ar-Rahman. How grievous will be the day for the infidels!

26.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
26.text: The true kingship on that day belongs to the Most Gracious; and that is hard for the disbelievers.

26.author: Arberry
26.text: the Kingdom that day, the true Kingdom, shall belong to the All-merciful and it shall be a day harsh for the unbelievers.

26.author: Daryabadi
26.text: The dominion on that Day rhall is; be the true dominion, of the Compassionate, l and it shall be anday upon the infidels hard.

26.author: Hilali & Khan
26.text: The sovereignty on that Day will be the true (sovereignty), belonging to the Most Beneficent (Allah), and it will be a hard Day for the disbelievers (those who disbelieve in the Oneness of Allah Islamic Monotheism).

26.author: Itani
26.text: On that Day, true sovereignty will belong to the Merciful, and it will be a difficult Day for the disbelievers.

26.author: Maududi
26.text: The real Kingdom on that Day will belong only to the Merciful, and it will be a very hard Day for the disbelievers.

26.author: Mubarakpuri
26.text: The sovereignty on that Day will be the true (sovereignty) of the Most Gracious, and it will be a hard Day for the disbelievers.

26.author: Pickthall
26.text: The Sovereignty on that day will be the True (Sovereignty) belonging to the Beneficent One, and it will be a hard day for disbelievers.

26.author: Qarai
26.text: on that day true sovereignty will belong to the All-beneficent, and it will be a hard day for the faithless.

26.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
26.text: the true Kingdom on that Day shall belong to the Merciful a harsh day for the unbelievers.

26.author: Saheeh International
26.text: True sovereignty, that Day, is for the Most Merciful. And it will be upon the disbelievers a difficult Day.

26.author: Sarwar
26.text: and the Absolute kingdom will belong to the Beneficent God.

26.author: Shakir
26.text: The kingdom on that day shall rightly belong to the Beneficent Allah, and a hard day shall it be for the unbelievers.

26.author: Wahiduddin Khan
26.text: true sovereignty on that Day will belong to the Gracious One, and it will be a hard Day for those who deny the truth.

26.author: Yusuf Ali
26.text: That Day, the dominion as of right and truth, shall be (wholly) for (Allah) Most Merciful: it will be a Day of dire difficulty for the misbelievers.

26.author: Transliteration
26.text: Almulku yawmaithin alhaqqu lilrrahmani wakana yawman AAala alkafireena AAaseeran

27.author: Ahmed Ali
27.text: The sinner will then bite his hand and say: "Would that I had taken the road with the Prophet.

27.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
27.text: And the day when the unjust will gnaw his hands, saying, “Alas, if only I had chosen a way along with the (Noble) Messenger (of Allah)!”

27.author: Arberry
27.text: Upon the day the evildoer shall bite his hands, saying, 'Would that I had taken a way along with the Messenger!

27.author: Daryabadi
27.text: On the Day when the wrong-doer shall gnaw his hands saying: would that had taken with the apostle a way!

27.author: Hilali & Khan
27.text: And (remember) the Day when the Zalim (wrong-doer, oppressor, polytheist, etc.) will bite at his hands, he will say: "Oh! Would that I had taken a path with the Messenger (Muhammad SAW).

27.author: Itani
27.text: On that Day, the wrongdoer will bite his hands, and say, “If only I had followed the way with the Messenger.

27.author: Maududi
27.text: The unjust man will bite at his hand and say, "Would that I had stood by the Messenger!

27.author: Mubarakpuri
27.text: And (remember) the Day when the wrongdoer will bite at his hands, he will say: "Oh! Would that I had taken a path with the Messenger."

27.author: Pickthall
27.text: On the day when the wrong-doer gnaweth his hands, he will say: Ah, would that I had chosen a way together with the messenger (of Allah)!

27.author: Qarai
27.text: It will be a day when the wrongdoer will bite his hands, saying, ‘I wish I had followed the Apostle’s way!

27.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
27.text: Upon that Day the harmdoer shall bite his hands, and say: 'Would that I had taken a Path with the Messenger!

27.author: Saheeh International
27.text: And the Day the wrongdoer will bite on his hands [in regret] he will say, "Oh, I wish I had taken with the Messenger a way.

27.author: Sarwar
27.text: It will be a hard day for the unbelievers. It will be a day when the unjust will bite their fingers, (regretfully) saying, "Would that we had followed the path of the Messengers.

27.author: Shakir
27.text: And the day when the unjust one shall bite his hands saying: O! would that I had taken a way with the Apostle

27.author: Wahiduddin Khan
27.text: On that Day, the wrongdoer will bite his hands and say, "Would that I had walked in the Messenger's path!
