27.author: Yusuf Ali
27.text: The Day that the wrong-doer will bite at his hands, he will say, "Oh! would that I had taken a (straight) path with the Messenger!

27.author: Transliteration
27.text: Wayawma yaAAaddu alththalimu AAala yadayhi yaqoolu ya laytanee ittakhathtu maAAa alrrasooli sabeelan

28.author: Ahmed Ali
28.text: Woe alas, ah would I had not taken so-and-so as friend!

28.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
28.text: “Woe to me – alas, if only I had not taken that one for a friend.”

28.author: Arberry
28.text: Alas, would that I had not taken So-and-so for a friend!

28.author: Daryabadi
28.text: Ah! woe unto me! wouldthat had never taken such a one for a friend!

28.author: Hilali & Khan
28.text: "Ah! Woe to me! Would that I had never taken so-and-so as a friend!

28.author: Itani
28.text: Oh, woe to me; I wish I never took so-and-so for a friend.

28.author: Maududi
28.text: O, woe to me! Would that I had not chosen so and so for a friend!

28.author: Mubarakpuri
28.text: "Ah! Woe to me! Would that I had never taken so-and-so as an intimate friend!"

28.author: Pickthall
28.text: Alas for me! Ah, would that I had never taken such an one for friend!

28.author: Qarai
28.text: Woe to me! I wish I had not taken so and so as a friend!

28.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
28.text: Would that I had never chosen soandso for my companion!

28.author: Saheeh International
28.text: Oh, woe to me! I wish I had not taken that one as a friend.

28.author: Sarwar
28.text: Woe to us! Would that we had not been friends with so and so.

28.author: Shakir
28.text: O woe is me! would that I had not taken such a one for a friend!

28.author: Wahiduddin Khan
28.text: Oh, would that I had never chosen such a one for my companion --

28.author: Yusuf Ali
28.text: "Ah! woe is me! Would that I had never taken such a one for a friend!

28.author: Transliteration
28.text: Ya waylata laytanee lam attakhith fulanan khaleelan

29.author: Ahmed Ali
29.text: He led me astray from the Warning after it had come to me. Satan always betrays man.

29.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
29.text: “He indeed led me astray from the advice that had come to me”; and Satan deserts man, leaving him unaided.

29.author: Arberry
29.text: He indeed led me astray from the Remembrance, after it had come to me; Satan is ever a forsaker of men.'

29.author: Daryabadi
29.text: Assuredly he misled me from the admonition after it had come unto ine. Verily the Satan is ever unto man a betrayer.

29.author: Hilali & Khan
29.text: "He indeed led me astray from the Reminder (this Quran) after it had come to me. And Shaitan (Satan) is ever a deserter to man in the hour of need."

29.author: Itani
29.text: He led me away from the Message after it had come to me; for Satan has always been a betrayer of man.”

29.author: Maududi
29.text: For it was he, who had deluded me to reject the Admonition which had come to me. Satan has proved very treacherous to man. "

29.author: Mubarakpuri
29.text: "He indeed led me astray from the Reminder after it had come to me. And Shaytan is to man ever a deserter."

29.author: Pickthall
29.text: He verily led me astray from the Reminder after it had reached me. Satan was ever man's deserter in the hour of need.

29.author: Qarai
29.text: Certainly he led me astray from the Reminder after it had come to me, and Satan is a deserter of man.’

29.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
29.text: He led me astray from the Remembrance after it had reached me, satan is ever the foresaker of humans'

29.author: Saheeh International
29.text: He led me away from the remembrance after it had come to me. And ever is Satan, to man, a deserter."

29.author: Sarwar
29.text: He led me away from the true guidance after it had come to us. Satan is a traitor to people."

29.author: Shakir
29.text: Certainly he led me astray from the reminder after it had come to me; and the Shaitan fails to aid man.

29.author: Wahiduddin Khan
29.text: he made me forgetful of the warning after it had reached me. Satan is man's great betrayer."

29.author: Yusuf Ali
29.text: "He did lead me astray from the Message (of Allah) after it had come to me! Ah! the Evil One is but a traitor to man!"

29.author: Transliteration
29.text: Laqad adallanee AAani alththikri baAAda ith jaanee wakana alshshaytanu lilinsani khathoolan

30.author: Ahmed Ali
30.text: The prophet will say: "O my Lord, my people had fettered the Qur'an."

30.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
30.text: And the Noble Messenger submitted, “O my Lord – indeed my people took this Qur’an as worthy of being abandoned.”

30.author: Arberry
30.text: The Messenger says, 'O my Lord, behold, my people have taken this Koran as a thing to be shunned.'

30.author: Daryabadi
30.text: And the Apostle will say: my lord! verily my people too k this Qur'an as a thing to be shunned.

30.author: Hilali & Khan
30.text: And the Messenger (Muhammad SAW) will say: "O my Lord! Verily, my people deserted this Quran (neither listened to it, nor acted on its laws and orders).

30.author: Itani
30.text: And the Messenger will say, “My Lord, my people have abandoned this Quran.”

30.author: Maududi
30.text: And the Messenger will say, "O my Lord, my people had made this Qur'an the object of their ridicule."

30.author: Mubarakpuri
30.text: And the Messenger will say: "O my Lord! Verily, my people deserted this Qur'an."

30.author: Pickthall
30.text: And the messenger saith: O my Lord! Lo! mine own folk make this Qur'an of no account.

30.author: Qarai
30.text: And the Apostle will say, ‘O my Lord! Indeed my people consigned this Quran to oblivion.’

30.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
30.text: The Messenger says: 'O my Lord, my people have taken this Koran while deserting it'

30.author: Saheeh International
30.text: And the Messenger has said, "O my Lord, indeed my people have taken this Qur'an as [a thing] abandoned."

30.author: Sarwar
30.text: The Messengers will say, "Lord, my people had abandoned this Quran."

30.author: Shakir
30.text: And the Apostle cried out: O my Lord! surely my people have treated this Quran as a forsaken thing.

30.author: Wahiduddin Khan
30.text: The Messenger will say, "Lord, my people did indeed discard the Quran,"

30.author: Yusuf Ali
30.text: Then the Messenger will say: "O my Lord! Truly my people took this Qur'an for just foolish nonsense."

30.author: Transliteration
30.text: Waqala alrrasoolu ya rabbi inna qawmee ittakhathoo hatha alqurana mahjooran

31.author: Ahmed Ali
31.text: Thus do We keep opponents among the sinners for every apostle; yet your Lord is sufficient as a guide and helper.

31.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
31.text: And this is how We had appointed guilty people as enemies to every Prophet; and Allah is Sufficient, to guide and to aid.

31.author: Arberry
31.text: Even so We have appointed to every Prophet an enemy among the sinners; but thy Lord suffices as a guide and as a helper.

31.author: Daryabadi
31.text: And even scals We appointed unto every prophet an enemy from among the culprits. And thine Lord sufficeth as GUide and Helper.

31.author: Hilali & Khan
31.text: Thus have We made for every Prophet an enemy among the Mujrimun (disbelievers, polytheists, criminals, etc.). But Sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper.

31.author: Itani
31.text: Likewise, to every prophet We assign enemies from among the wicked. But your Lord suffices as a Guide and Savior.

31.author: Maududi
31.text: O Muhammad, in this very way We have made the criminals the enemies of every Prophet, but your Lord suffices for you as your Guide and Helper.

31.author: Mubarakpuri
31.text: Thus have We made for every Prophet an enemy among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and Helper.

31.author: Pickthall
31.text: Even so have We appointed unto every prophet an opponent from among the guilty; but Allah sufficeth for a Guide and Helper.

31.author: Qarai
31.text: That is how for every prophet We assigned an enemy from among the guilty, and your Lord suffices as helper and guide.

31.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
31.text: To every Prophet We have appointed an enemy among the harmdoers; your Lord is Sufficient for you, a Guide and a Helper.

31.author: Saheeh International
31.text: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the criminals. But sufficient is your Lord as a guide and a helper.

31.author: Sarwar
31.text: Thus, from the sinful people We made enemies for every Prophet. Your Lord is a Sufficient Guide and Helper.

31.author: Shakir
31.text: And thus have We made for every prophet an enemy from among the sinners and sufficient is your Lord as a Guide and a Helper.

31.author: Wahiduddin Khan
31.text: thus did We assign to every prophet an enemy from among the sinners; your Lord is sufficient as a guide and a helper.

31.author: Yusuf Ali
31.text: Thus have We made for every prophet an enemy among the sinners: but enough is thy Lord to guide and to help.

31.author: Transliteration
31.text: Wakathalika jaAAalna likulli nabiyyin AAaduwwan mina almujrimeena wakafa birabbika hadiyan wanaseeran

32.author: Ahmed Ali
32.text: The unbelievers say: "Why was the whole Qur'an not sent down all at once to him?" It was sent thus that We may keep your heart resolute. So We enunciated it by steps and distinctly.

32.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
32.text: And the disbelievers say, “Why was the Qur’an not sent down to him all at once?” This is how We sent it in stages, in order to strengthen your heart with it and We read it slowly, in stages.

32.author: Arberry
32.text: The unbelievers say, 'Why has the Koran not been sent down upon him all at once?' Even so, that We may strengthen thy heart thereby, and We have chanted it very distinctly.

32.author: Daryabadi
32.text: And those who disbelieve say: wherefore is the Qur'an not revealed unto him entire at once! Thus---We reveal- that We may establish thy heart there with; and We have repeated it with a repetition.

32.author: Hilali & Khan
32.text: And those who disbelieve say: "Why is not the Quran revealed to him all at once?" Thus (it is sent down in parts), that We may strengthen your heart thereby. And We have revealed it to you gradually, in stages. (It was revealed to the Prophet SAW in 23 years.).

32.author: Itani
32.text: Those who disbelieve say, “Why was the Quran not revealed to him at once?” Thus in order to strengthen your heart thereby, and We revealed it in stages.

32.author: Maududi
32.text: The disbelievers say, "Why has not the entire Qur'an been sent down to this man all at nce?" -Well, this has been done to impress it deeply on your mind, and (for the same object) We have sent it down piecemeal by degrees.

32.author: Mubarakpuri
32.text: And those who disbelieve say: "Why is not the Qur'an revealed to him all at once" Thus, that We may strengthen your heart thereby. And We have revealed it to you gradually, in stages.

32.author: Pickthall
32.text: And those who disbelieve say: Why is the Qur'an not revealed unto him all at once? (It is revealed) thus that We may strengthen thy heart therewith; and We have arranged it in right order.

32.author: Qarai
32.text: The faithless say, ‘Why has not the Quran been sent down to him all at once?’ So it was, that We may strengthen your heart with it, and We have recited it [to you] in a measured tone.

32.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
32.text: The unbelievers ask: 'Why was the Koran not sent down to him all at once' As such We strengthen your heart thereby, and We have recited it very distinctly.

32.author: Saheeh International
32.text: And those who disbelieve say, "Why was the Qur'an not revealed to him all at once?" Thus [it is] that We may strengthen thereby your heart. And We have spaced it distinctly.

32.author: Sarwar
32.text: The unbelievers have said, "Why was the whole Quran not revealed to him at once?" We have revealed it to you in gradual steps to strengthen your hearts and give you explanations.

32.author: Shakir
32.text: And those who disbelieve say: Why has not the Quran been revealed to him all at once? Thus, that We may strengthen your heart by it and We have arranged it well in arranging.

32.author: Wahiduddin Khan
32.text: Those who deny the truth say, "Why was the Quran not sent down to him in a single revelation?" We sent it in this manner, so that We might strengthen your heart. We gave it to you in gradual revelation.

32.author: Yusuf Ali
32.text: Those who reject Faith say: "Why is not the Qur'an revealed to him all at once? Thus (is it revealed), that We may strengthen thy heart thereby, and We have rehearsed it to thee in slow, well-arranged stages, gradually.

32.author: Transliteration
32.text: Waqala allatheena kafaroo lawla nuzzila AAalayhi alquranu jumlatan wahidatan kathalika linuthabbita bihi fuadaka warattalnahu tarteelan

33.author: Ahmed Ali
33.text: There is not an example they advance to which We do not give you a right answer and a better explanation.

33.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
33.text: And they will never bring you an example, but We shall bring you a true narration and better than it.

33.author: Arberry
33.text: They bring not to thee any similitude but that We bring thee the truth, and better in exposition.

33.author: Daryabadi
33.text: And they come not unto thee with a similitude but We bring thee the truth and an excellent interpretation.

33.author: Hilali & Khan
33.text: And no example or similitude do they bring (to oppose or to find fault in you or in this Quran), but We reveal to you the truth (against that similitude or example), and the better explanation thereof.

33.author: Itani
33.text: Whatever argument they come to you with, We provide you with the truth, and a better exposition.

33.author: Maududi
33.text: And (there is another wisdom in this: whenever they brought to you an odd thing (or a strange question), We sent its right answer to you in time and explained it all in the best manner,

33.author: Mubarakpuri
33.text: And no example or parable do they bring, but We reveal to you the truth, and the better explanation thereof.

33.author: Pickthall
33.text: And they bring thee no similitude but We bring thee the Truth (as against it), and better (than their similitude) as argument.

33.author: Qarai
33.text: They do not bring you any representation but that We bring you the truth [in reply to them] and the best exposition.

33.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
33.text: They do not bring to you any parable but that which We bring to you is the truth and better in explanation.

33.author: Saheeh International
33.text: And they do not come to you with an argument except that We bring you the truth and the best explanation.

33.author: Sarwar
33.text: We will support you with the Truth and the best interpretation whenever the infidels argue against you.

33.author: Shakir
33.text: And they shall not bring to you any argument, but We have brought to you (one) with truth and best in significance.

33.author: Wahiduddin Khan
33.text: Every time they raise an objection, We will bring you the truth and the best of explanations.

33.author: Yusuf Ali
33.text: And no question do they bring to thee but We reveal to thee the truth and the best explanation (thereof).

33.author: Transliteration
33.text: Wala yatoonaka bimathalin illa jinaka bialhaqqi waahsana tafseeran

34.author: Ahmed Ali
34.text: Those who will be pushed faces forward into Hell will be in a worse position, farther away from the path.

34.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
34.text: Those who will be dragged on their faces towards hell – theirs is the worst destination and they are the most astray.

34.author: Arberry
34.text: Those who shall be mustered to Gehenna upon their faces -- they shall be worse in place, and gone further astray from the way.

34.author: Daryabadi
34.text: They who shall be gathered prone on their faces unto Hell-- those shall be worst in respect of place and the most astray in respect of path.

34.author: Hilali & Khan
34.text: Those who will be gathered to Hell (prone) on their faces, such will be in an evil state, and most astray from the (Straight) Path.

34.author: Itani
34.text: Those who are herded into Hell on their faces—those are in a worse position, and further astray from the way.

34.author: Maududi
34.text: those who are going to be driven to Hell upon their faces, have taken an utterly wrong stand and their way is most erroneous!

34.author: Mubarakpuri
34.text: Those who will be gathered to Hell on their faces, such will be in an evil state, and most astray from the path.

34.author: Pickthall
34.text: Those who will be gathered on their faces unto hell: such are worse in plight and further from the right road.

34.author: Qarai
34.text: Those who will be mustered [fallen] on their faces toward hell, they are the worse situated and further astray from the [right] way.

34.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
34.text: Those who will be gathered into Gehenna (Hell) upon their faces shall be in the worst in place, and have gone further astray from the Path.

34.author: Saheeh International
34.text: The ones who are gathered on their faces to Hell - those are the worst in position and farthest astray in [their] way.

34.author: Sarwar
34.text: Those who will be driven headlong into hell will have a terrible dwelling; they have certainly gone astray.

34.author: Shakir
34.text: (As for) those who shall be gathered upon their faces to hell, they are in a worse plight and straying farther away from the path.

34.author: Wahiduddin Khan
34.text: Those who will be dragged headlong into Hell shall have an evil place to dwell in, for they have strayed far from the right path.

34.author: Yusuf Ali
34.text: Those who will be gathered to Hell (prone) on their faces,- they will be in an evil plight, and, as to Path, most astray.

34.author: Transliteration
34.text: Allatheena yuhsharoona AAala wujoohihim ila jahannama olaika sharrun makanan waadallu sabeelan

35.author: Ahmed Ali
35.text: We gave Moses the Book, and made his brother Aaron his minister,

35.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
35.text: And indeed We gave Moosa the Book and appointed with him his brother Haroon as a deputy.

35.author: Arberry
35.text: We gave Moses the Book, and appointed with him his brother Aaron as minister

35.author: Daryabadi
35.text: And assuredly We vouchsafed unto Musa the Book and We placed with him his brother Harun as a minister.

35.author: Hilali & Khan
35.text: And indeed We gave Musa (Moses) the Scripture [the Taurat (Torah)], and placed his brother Harun (Aaron) with him as a helper;

35.author: Itani
35.text: We gave Moses the Scripture, and appointed his brother Aaron as his assistant.

35.author: Maududi
35.text: We gave Moses the Book and appointed his brother Aaron as his counsellor:

35.author: Mubarakpuri
35.text: And indeed We gave Musa the Scripture, and placed his brother Harun with him as a helper;

35.author: Pickthall
35.text: We verily gave Moses the Scripture and placed with him his brother Aaron as henchman.

35.author: Qarai
35.text: Certainly We gave Moses the Book and We made Aaron, his brother, accompany him as a minister.

35.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
35.text: We gave the Book to Moses and gave him his brother Aaron as a minister.

35.author: Saheeh International
35.text: And We had certainly given Moses the Scripture and appointed with him his brother Aaron as an assistant.

35.author: Sarwar
35.text: We gave the Book to Moses and made his brother Aaron his Minister.

35.author: Shakir
35.text: And certainly We gave Musa the Book and We appointed with him his brother Haroun an aider.

35.author: Wahiduddin Khan
35.text: We gave Moses the Book, and appointed his brother Aaron as his supporter.

35.author: Yusuf Ali
35.text: (Before this,) We sent Moses The Book, and appointed his brother Aaron with him as minister;

35.author: Transliteration
35.text: Walaqad atayna moosa alkitaba wajaAAalna maAAahu akhahu haroona wazeeran

36.author: Ahmed Ali
36.text: Then We told them: "Go to the people who have rejected Our signs." Then We annihilated them completely.

36.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
36.text: We therefore said, “Go, both of you, to the people who have denied Our signs”; then ruining the people, We destroyed them completely.

36.author: Arberry
36.text: and We said, 'Go to the people who have cried lies to Our signs'; then We destroyed them utterly.

36.author: Daryabadi
36.text: Then We said: go ye twain unto a people who have belied Our signs. Then We annihilated them an utter annihilation.

36.author: Hilali & Khan
36.text: And We said: "Go you both to the people who have denied Our Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.)." Then We destroyed them with utter destruction.

36.author: Itani
36.text: We said, “Go to the people who rejected Our signs,” and We destroyed them completely.

36.author: Maududi
36.text: then We said to them, "Go to the people who have treated Our Revelations as false. " So We annihilated those people utterly.

36.author: Mubarakpuri
36.text: And We said: "Go you both to the people who have denied Our Ayat." Then We destroyed them with utter destruction.

36.author: Pickthall
36.text: Then We said: Go together unto the folk who have denied Our revelations. Then We destroyed them, a complete destruction.

36.author: Qarai
36.text: Then We said, ‘Let the two of you go to the people who have denied Our signs.’ Then We destroyed them utterly.

36.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
36.text: (Then) We said: 'Go to the nation who have belied Our signs' And We utterly destroyed them.

36.author: Saheeh International
36.text: And We said, "Go both of you to the people who have denied Our signs." Then We destroyed them with [complete] destruction.

36.author: Sarwar
36.text: We told them, "Both of you go to the people who have rejected Our revelations." We completely destroyed these unbelievers.

36.author: Shakir
36.text: Then We said: Go you both to the people who rejected Our communications; so We destroyed them with utter destruction.

36.author: Wahiduddin Khan
36.text: Then We said, "Go together to the people who have denied Our signs." We utterly destroyed them!

36.author: Yusuf Ali
36.text: And We command: "Go ye both, to the people who have rejected our Signs:" And those (people) We destroyed with utter destruction.

36.author: Transliteration
36.text: Faqulna ithhaba ila alqawmi allatheena kaththaboo biayatina fadammarnahum tadmeeran

37.author: Ahmed Ali
37.text: We had drowned the people of Noah when they had accused the apostles of lies, and turned them into an example for men. We have prepared a painful punishment for the wicked.

37.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
37.text: And the people of Nooh – when they denied the Noble Messengers, We drowned them and made them a sign for mankind; and We have kept prepared a painful punishment for the unjust.

37.author: Arberry
37.text: And the people of Noah, when they cried lies to the Messengers, We drowned them, and made them to be a sign to mankind; and We have prepared for the evildoers a painful chastisement.

37.author: Daryabadi
37.text: And the people of Nuh! When they belied the apostles We drowned them, and made them a sign unto mankind. And We have gotten ready unto the wrong-doers a torment afflictive,

37.author: Hilali & Khan
37.text: And Nuh's (Noah) people, when they denied the Messengers We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the Zalimun (polytheists and wrong-doers, etc).

37.author: Itani
37.text: And the people of Noah: when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them, and made them a lesson for mankind. We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful retribution.

37.author: Maududi
37.text: The same was the case with the people of Noah when they charged the Messenger with imposture: We drowned them and made them a sign of warning for entire mankind, and We have prepared a painful chastisement for the unjust.

37.author: Mubarakpuri
37.text: And Nuh's people, when they denied the Messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a sign for mankind. And We have prepared a painful torment for the wrongdoers.

37.author: Pickthall
37.text: And Noah's folk, when they denied the messengers, We drowned them and made of them a portent for mankind. We have prepared a painful doom for evil-doers.

37.author: Qarai
37.text: And Noah’s people, We drowned them when they impugned the apostles, and We made them a sign for mankind, and We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.

37.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
37.text: The nation of Noah, We drowned them when they belied their Messenger, and made of them a sign to the nation. For the harmdoers We have prepared a painful punishment

37.author: Saheeh International
37.text: And the people of Noah - when they denied the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them for mankind a sign. And We have prepared for the wrongdoers a painful punishment.

37.author: Sarwar
37.text: We drowned the people of Noah because of their rejection of the Messengers and made them evidence of the Truth for mankind. We have prepared a painful torment for the unjust ones.

37.author: Shakir
37.text: And the people of Nuh, when they rejected the apostles, We drowned them, and made them a sign for men, and We have prepared a painful punishment for the unjust;

37.author: Wahiduddin Khan
37.text: We drowned the people of Noah also when they rejected their messengers and We made them an example to all mankind. We have prepared a painful punishment for the wrongdoers,

37.author: Yusuf Ali
37.text: And the people of Noah,- when they rejected the messengers, We drowned them, and We made them as a Sign for mankind; and We have prepared for (all) wrong-doers a grievous Penalty;-

37.author: Transliteration
37.text: Waqawma noohin lamma kaththaboo alrrusula aghraqnahum wajaAAalnahum lilnnasi ayatan waaAAtadna lilththalimeena AAathaban aleeman

38.author: Ahmed Ali
38.text: (As for) 'Ad, Thamud and the people of ar-Rass, and many generations in between them,

38.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
38.text: And the tribes of A’ad and the Thamud, and the people of the Wells, and many a generation between them.

38.author: Arberry
38.text: And Ad, and Thamood, and the men of Er-Rass, and between that generations a many,

38.author: Daryabadi
38.text: And the 'Aad and the Thamud and the dwellers of the pass and generations in-between many.

38.author: Hilali & Khan
38.text: And (also) 'Ad and Thamud, and the dwellers of Ar-Rass, and many generations in between.

38.author: Itani
38.text: And Aad, and Thamood, and the inhabitants of Arras, and many generations in between.

38.author: Maududi
38.text: Likewise were destroyed the `Ad and the Thamud and the people of the Rass, and many a generation in between.

38.author: Mubarakpuri
38.text: And (also) `Ad and Thamud, and the Dwellers of Ar-Rass, and many generations in between.

38.author: Pickthall
38.text: And (the tribes of) A'ad and Thamud, and the dwellers in Ar-Rass, and many generations in between.

38.author: Qarai
38.text: And ‘Ad and Thamud, and the people of Rass, and many generations between them.

38.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
38.text: also to Aad and Thamood and the nation of ErRass and many generations in between;

38.author: Saheeh International
38.text: And [We destroyed] 'Aad and Thamud and the companions of the well and many generations between them.

38.author: Sarwar
38.text: To each of the tribes of Ad, Thamud, the settlers around the well and many generations in between

38.author: Shakir
38.text: And Ad and Samood and the dwellers of the Rass and many generations between them.

38.author: Wahiduddin Khan
38.text: to  'Ad, and Thamud, and the people of al-Rass, and as We did for the many a generation between them.

38.author: Yusuf Ali
38.text: As also 'Ad and Thamud, and the Companions of the Rass, and many a generation between them.

38.author: Transliteration
38.text: WaAAadan wathamooda waashaba alrrassi waquroonan bayna thalika katheeran

39.author: Ahmed Ali
39.text: We administered warnings to each of them, and then destroyed them completely.

39.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
39.text: And We illustrated examples to each one of them; and ruining them, annihilated them all.

39.author: Arberry
39.text: for each We struck similitudes, and each We ruined utterly.

39.author: Daryabadi
39.text: And unto each! We propounded similitude thereunto; and each We ruined an utter ruin.

39.author: Hilali & Khan
39.text: And for each of them We put forward examples (as proofs and lessons, etc.), and each (of them) We brought to utter ruin (because of their disbelief and evil deeds).

39.author: Itani
39.text: To each We presented the parables; and each We devastated utterly.

39.author: Maududi
39.text: We admonished each one of them by citing the examples (of those who were destroyed before them) and ultimately annihilated all of them.

39.author: Mubarakpuri
39.text: And for each We put forward examples, and each We brought to utter ruin.

39.author: Pickthall
39.text: Each (of them) We warned by examples, and each (of them) We brought to utter ruin.

39.author: Qarai
39.text: For each of them We drew examples, and each We destroyed utterly.

39.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
39.text: to each of them We gave examples, and each of them We utterly ruined.

39.author: Saheeh International
39.text: And for each We presented examples [as warnings], and each We destroyed with [total] destruction.

39.author: Sarwar
39.text: We gave guidance and drove each to destruction.

39.author: Shakir
39.text: And to every one We gave examples and every one did We destroy with utter destruction.

39.author: Wahiduddin Khan
39.text: To each of them We gave warnings and each of them We destroyed completely.

39.author: Yusuf Ali
39.text: To each one We set forth Parables and examples; and each one We broke to utter annihilation (for their sins).

39.author: Transliteration
39.text: Wakullan darabna lahu alamthala wakullan tabbarna tatbeeran

40.author: Ahmed Ali
40.text: They must have surely passed by the town on which We had rained the terrible rain of ruin. How could they not have seen it? Still they do not dread the Resurrection.

40.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
40.text: And indeed they have visited the township upon which had rained a harmful rain; so were they not seeing it? In fact, they never expected to be raised again.

40.author: Arberry
40.text: Surely they have come by the city that was rained on by an evil rain; what, have they not seen it? Nay, but they look for no upraising.

40.author: Daryabadi
40.text: And assuredly they have gone by the township whereon was rained the evil rain. Are they not wont to see it? Aye! they expect not Resurrection.

40.author: Hilali & Khan
40.text: And indeed they have passed by the town [of Prophet Lout (Lot)] on which was rained the evil rain. Did they (disbelievers) not then see it (with their own eyes)? Nay! But they used not to expect for any resurrection.

40.author: Itani
40.text: And they came upon the city that was drenched by the terrible rain. Did they not see it? But they do not expect resurrection.

40.author: Maududi
40.text: Surely, these people have passed by that habitation on which was rained an evil rain: have they not seen its ruins? But they do not expect another life in the Hereafter.

40.author: Mubarakpuri
40.text: And indeed they have passed by the town on which was rained the evil rain. Did they not then see it Nay! But they used not to expect any resurrection.

40.author: Pickthall
40.text: And indeed they have passed by the township whereon was rained the fatal rain. Can it be that they have not seen it? Nay, but they hope for no resurrection.

40.author: Qarai
40.text: Certainly they must have passed the town on which an evil shower was rained. Have they not seen it? Rather, they did not expect resurrection [to happen].

40.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
40.text: They have surely passed by the village which was rained upon by evil rain (of stones); what, have they never seen it? No, they look for no resurrection.

40.author: Saheeh International
40.text: And they have already come upon the town which was showered with a rain of evil. So have they not seen it? But they are not expecting resurrection.

40.author: Sarwar
40.text: Our Messengers came into the town which was struck by a fatal rain. Did they (unbelievers), not see what had happened to this town? In fact, they had no faith in the Resurrection.

40.author: Shakir
40.text: And certainly they have (often) passed by the town on which was rained an evil rain; did they not then see it? Nay! they did not hope to be raised again.

40.author: Wahiduddin Khan
40.text: Indeed they must have come upon the town on which an evil rain had poured down. Did they not see it? Yet they have no faith in the Resurrection.

40.author: Yusuf Ali
40.text: And the (Unbelievers) must indeed have passed by the town on which was rained a shower of evil: did they not then see it (with their own eyes)? But they fear not the Resurrection.

40.author: Transliteration
40.text: Walaqad ataw AAala alqaryati allatee omtirat matara alssawi afalam yakoonoo yarawnaha bal kanoo la yarjoona nushooran

41.author: Ahmed Ali
41.text: When they see you they take you only in jest: "Is this the one whom God has sent as messenger?

41.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
41.text: And when they see you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) they take you but as a mockery, “Is this the one whom Allah sent as a (Noble) Messenger?”

41.author: Arberry
41.text: And when they see thee, they take thee in mockery only: 'What, is this he whom God sent forth as a Messenger?

41.author: Daryabadi
41.text: And when they behold thee, they hold thee up for mockery: is this the one whom Allah hath sent as an apostle

41.author: Hilali & Khan
41.text: And when they see you (O Muhammad SAW), they treat you only as a mockery (saying):"Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a Messenger?

41.author: Itani
41.text: And when they see you, they take you for nothing but mockery: “Is this the one God sent as a messenger?”

41.author: Maududi
41.text: When these people see you, they scoff at you, (saying), "Is this the man whom Allah has sent as His Messenger?

41.author: Mubarakpuri
41.text: And when they see you, they treat you only in mockery (saying): "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a Messenger"

41.author: Pickthall
41.text: And when they see thee (O Muhammad) they treat thee only as a jest (saying): Is this he whom Allah sendeth as a messenger?

41.author: Qarai
41.text: When they see you they just take you in derision: ‘Is this the one whom Allah has sent as an apostle!?

41.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
41.text: Whenever they see you, they mock you (saying): 'Is this whom Allah has sent as a Messenger?

41.author: Saheeh International
41.text: And when they see you, [O Muhammad], they take you not except in ridicule, [saying], "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger?

41.author: Sarwar
41.text: (Muhammad), when they see you, they will only mock you and say, "Has God really sent him as a Messengers?

41.author: Shakir
41.text: And when they see you, they do not take you for aught but a mockery: Is this he whom Allah has raised to be an apostle?

41.author: Wahiduddin Khan
41.text: Whenever they see you they only make a mockery of you -- "Is this the one God has sent as His Messenger?

41.author: Yusuf Ali
41.text: When they see thee, they treat thee no otherwise than in mockery: "Is this the one whom Allah has sent as a messenger?"

41.author: Transliteration
41.text: Waitha raawka in yattakhithoonaka illa huzuwan ahatha allathee baAAatha Allahu rasoolan

42.author: Ahmed Ali
42.text: He would have surely turned us away from our gods if we had not adhered to them." They will know soon who is farther away from the path when they see the punishment!

42.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
42.text: “Possibly he would have misled us away from our Gods had we not been firm upon them”; soon they will know, when they see the punishment, who was astray from the path.

42.author: Arberry
42.text: Wellnigh he had led us astray from our gods, but that we kept steadfast to them.' Assuredly they shall know, when they see the chastisement, who is further astray from the way.

42.author: Daryabadi
42.text: Well nigh he had led us astray from our gods if we had not persevered towards them. Presently they shall know, When they behold the torment, who, more astray in respect of path.

42.author: Hilali & Khan
42.text: "He would have nearly misled us from our aliha (gods), had it not been that we were patient and constant in their worship!" And they will know when they see the torment, who it is that is most astray from the (Right) Path!

42.author: Itani
42.text: “He nearly led us away from our gods, had we not patiently adhered to them.” But they will know, when they witness the torment, who is further away from the way.

42.author: Maududi
42.text: He had almost led us astray from our gods, had we not remained firm in our faith with regard to them. " The time is not far when they will see the torment and realize who had strayed far from the truth.

42.author: Mubarakpuri
42.text: "He would have nearly misled us from our gods, had it not been that we were patient and constant in their worship!" And they will know, when they see the torment, who it is that is most astray from the path!

42.author: Pickthall
42.text: He would have led us far away from our gods if we had not been staunch to them. They will know, when they behold the doom, who is more astray as to the road.

42.author: Qarai
42.text: Indeed he was about to lead us astray from our gods, had we not stood firm by them.’ Soon they will know, when they sight the punishment, who is further astray from the [right] way.

42.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
42.text: He would have mislead us from our gods, if we had not been steadfast to them' But they shall know who is further astray from the Path when they see the punishment.

42.author: Saheeh International
42.text: He almost would have misled us from our gods had we not been steadfast in [worship of] them." But they are going to know, when they see the punishment, who is farthest astray in [his] way.

42.author: Sarwar
42.text: Had we not been steadfast he would almost have led us astray from our gods." On facing torment they will soon know who had really gone astray.

42.author: Shakir
42.text: He had well-nigh led us astray from our gods had we not adhered to them patiently! And they will know, when they see the punishment, who is straying farther off from the path.

42.author: Wahiduddin Khan
42.text: Indeed, he would well-nigh have led us astray from our deities, had we not been [so] steadfastly attached to them!" When they behold the punishment, they shall realize who strayed furthest from the right path.

42.author: Yusuf Ali
42.text: "He indeed would well-nigh have misled us from our gods, had it not been that we were constant to them!" - Soon will they know, when they see the Penalty, who it is that is most misled in Path!

42.author: Transliteration
42.text: In kada layudilluna AAan alihatina lawla an sabarna AAalayha wasawfa yaAAlamoona heena yarawna alAAathaba man adallu sabeelan

43.author: Ahmed Ali
43.text: Have you considered him who takes his own lust for his god? Can you stand a surety for him?

43.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
43.text: Did you see the one who chose his own desires as his God? So will you accept the responsibility of guarding him?

43.author: Arberry
43.text: Hast thou seen him who has taken his caprice to be his god? Wilt thou be a guardian over them?

43.author: Daryabadi
43.text: Hast thou observed him who hath taken as his god his own desire! wilt thou be over him a trustee?

43.author: Hilali & Khan
43.text: Have you (O Muhammad SAW) seen him who has taken as his ilah (god) his own desire? Would you then be a Wakil (a disposer of his affairs or a watcher) over him?

43.author: Itani
43.text: Have you seen him who chose his desire as his god? Would you be an agent for him?

43.author: Maududi
43.text: Have you ever considered the case of the person who has made his lust his god? Can you take the responsibility of guiding such a one aright?

43.author: Mubarakpuri
43.text: Have you seen him who has taken as his god his own vain desire Would you then be a guardian over him

43.author: Pickthall
43.text: Hast thou seen him who chooseth for his god his own lust? Wouldst thou then be guardian over him?

43.author: Qarai
43.text: Have you seen him who has taken his desire to be his god? Is it your duty to watch over him?

43.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
43.text: Have you seen him who has made gods of his own desires? Would you be a guardian over him?

43.author: Saheeh International
43.text: Have you seen the one who takes as his god his own desire? Then would you be responsible for him?

43.author: Sarwar
43.text: How can you be the guardian of those who have chosen their own desires as their Lord?

43.author: Shakir
43.text: Have you seen him who takes his low desires for his god? Will you then be a protector over him?

43.author: Wahiduddin Khan
43.text: Have you seen him who has taken his own desire to be his god? Can you be a guardian over him?

43.author: Yusuf Ali
43.text: Seest thou such a one as taketh for his god his own passion (or impulse)? Couldst thou be a disposer of affairs for him?

43.author: Transliteration
43.text: Araayta mani ittakhatha ilahahu hawahu afaanta takoonu AAalayhi wakeelan

44.author: Ahmed Ali
44.text: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are no better than cattle; in fact they are farther astray from the path.

44.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
44.text: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand something? They are not but like the cattle – in fact more astray from the path than them!

44.author: Arberry
44.text: Or deemest thou that most of them hear or understand? They are but as the cattle; nay, they are further astray from the way.

44.author: Daryabadi
44.text: Or deemest thou that most of them hear or understand? They are but like unto the cattle; nay, they are even farther astray from the path.

44.author: Hilali & Khan
44.text: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like cattle; nay, they are even farther astray from the Path. (i.e. even worst than cattle).

44.author: Itani
44.text: Or do you assume that most of them hear or understand? They are just like cattle, but even more errant in their way.

44.author: Maududi
44.text: Do you think that most of them hear or understand? They are only like the cattle; nay, even worse than the cattle.

44.author: Mubarakpuri
44.text: Or do you think that most of them hear or understand They are only like cattle -- nay, they are even farther astray from the path.

44.author: Pickthall
44.text: Or deemest thou that most of them hear or understand? They are but as the cattle - nay, but they are farther astray?

44.author: Qarai
44.text: Do you suppose that most of them listen or exercise their reason? They are just like cattle; indeed, they are further astray from the way.

44.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
44.text: Do you think that most of them can hear or understand? They are like cattle, no, they are further astray from the Path.

44.author: Saheeh International
44.text: Or do you think that most of them hear or reason? They are not except like livestock. Rather, they are [even] more astray in [their] way.

44.author: Sarwar
44.text: Do you think that most of them listen and understand? They are like cattle or even more, straying and confused.

44.author: Shakir
44.text: Or do you think that most of them do hear or understand? They are nothing but as cattle; nay, they are straying farther off from the path.

44.author: Wahiduddin Khan
44.text: Do you think most of them can hear or understand? They are like cattle. Indeed, they are even more astray.

44.author: Yusuf Ali
44.text: Or thinkest thou that most of them listen or understand? They are only like cattle;- nay, they are worse astray in Path.

44.author: Transliteration
44.text: Am tahsabu anna aktharahum yasmaAAoona aw yaAAqiloona in hum illa kaalanAAami bal hum adallu sabeelan

45.author: Ahmed Ali
45.text: Have you not seen how your Lord lengthens out the shadow? He could have kept it motionless if He liked. Yet We make the sun its pilot to show the way.

45.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
45.text: O dear Prophet, did you not see your Lord, how He spread the shade? And if He willed, He could have made it still; then We made the sun a proof upon it.

45.author: Arberry
45.text: Hast thou not regarded thy Lord, how He has stretched out the shadow? Had He willed, He would have made it still. Then We appointed the sun, to be a guide to it;

45.author: Daryabadi
45.text: Hast thou not observed thine lord-how He hath stretched out the shadow? And if He had willed He would have made it still. Then We have made the sun for it an indication.

45.author: Hilali & Khan
45.text: Have you not seen how your Lord spread the shadow. If He willed, He could have made it still then We have made the sun its guide [i.e. after the sunrise, it (the shadow) squeezes and vanishes at midnoon and then again appears in the afternoon with the decline of the sun, and had there been no sun light, there would have been no shadow].

45.author: Itani
45.text: Do you not see how your Lord extends the shadow? Had He willed, He could have made it still. And We made the sun a pointer to it.

45.author: Maududi
45.text: Have you not seen how your Lord lengthens out the shadow? Had He willed, He would have made it constant, but We have made the sun its pilot;

45.author: Mubarakpuri
45.text: Have you not seen how your Lord spread the shadow. If He willed, He could have made it still -- but We have made the sun its guide.

45.author: Pickthall
45.text: Hast thou not seen how thy Lord hath spread the shade - And if He willed He could have made it still - then We have made the sun its pilot;

45.author: Qarai
45.text: Have you not regarded how your Lord spreads the twilight? (Had He wished He would have made it stand still.) Then We made the sun a beacon for it.

45.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
45.text: Do you not see how your Lord stretches the shadow? Had it been His will, He could have made it constant. Then He appointed the sun to be a guide to it;

45.author: Saheeh International
45.text: Have you not considered your Lord - how He extends the shadow, and if He willed, He could have made it stationary? Then We made the sun for it an indication.

45.author: Sarwar
45.text: Have you not seen that your Lord increases the shadow. Had He wanted He would have made it stationary], and has made the sun their guide.

45.author: Shakir
45.text: Have you not considered (the work of) your Lord, how He extends the shade? And if He had pleased He would certainly have made it stationary; then We have made the sun an indication of it

45.author: Wahiduddin Khan
45.text: Have you not seen how your Lord lengthens the shadows? Had He pleased, He could have made them constant; then We placed the sun as an indicator for them,

45.author: Yusuf Ali
45.text: Hast thou not turned thy vision to thy Lord?- How He doth prolong the shadow! If He willed, He could make it stationary! then do We make the sun its guide;

45.author: Transliteration
45.text: Alam tara ila rabbika kayfa madda alththilla walaw shaa lajaAAalahu sakinan thumma jaAAalna alshshamsa AAalayhi daleelan

46.author: Ahmed Ali
46.text: Then We draw it back to Us, withdrawing it little by little.

46.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
46.text: We then gradually withdraw it towards Us.

46.author: Arberry
46.text: thereafter We seize it to Ourselves, drawing it gently.

46.author: Daryabadi
46.text: Then We draw it toward Us with an easy drawing.

46.author: Hilali & Khan
46.text: Then We withdraw it to Us a gradual concealed withdrawal.

46.author: Itani
46.text: Then We withdraw it towards Us gradually.

46.author: Maududi
46.text: then (as the sun climbs up), We roll it up little by little towards Ourselves.

46.author: Mubarakpuri
46.text: Then We withdraw it towards Ourselves -- a gradual withdrawal.

46.author: Pickthall
46.text: Then We withdraw it unto Us, a gradual withdrawal?

46.author: Qarai
46.text: Then We retract it toward Ourselves, with a gentle retracting.

46.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
46.text: thereafter We seize it to Us withdrawing it gently.

46.author: Saheeh International
46.text: Then We hold it in hand for a brief grasp.

46.author: Sarwar
46.text: Then He reduces it in gradual steps.

46.author: Shakir
46.text: Then We take it to Ourselves, taking little by little.

46.author: Wahiduddin Khan
46.text: then We withdrew it to Us, a gradual withdrawal.

46.author: Yusuf Ali
46.text: Then We draw it in towards Ourselves,- a contraction by easy stages.

47.author: Ahmed Ali
47.text: It is He who made the night a covering for you; and made sleep for rest, the day for rising.

47.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
47.text: And it is He Who made the night a veil for you, and the sleep a rest, and made the day for getting up.

47.author: Arberry
47.text: It is He who appointed the night for you to be a garment and sleep for a rest, and day He appointed for a rising.

47.author: Daryabadi
47.text: And it is He who hath made for you the night a covering, and the sleep; repose, and hath made the day a resurrection.

47.author: Hilali & Khan
47.text: And it is He Who makes the night a covering for you, and the sleep (as) repose, and makes the day Nushur (i.e. getting up and going about here and there for daily work, etc. after one's sleep at night or like resurrection after one's death).

47.author: Itani
47.text: And it is He who made the night a covering for you, and sleep for rest; and He made the day a revival.

47.author: Maududi
47.text: And it is Allah Who has ordained the night as a garment for you, and the sleep as a repose of death, and the day as the time of return to life.

47.author: Mubarakpuri
47.text: And it is He Who makes the night a covering for you, and the sleep (as) a repose, and makes the day Nushur.

47.author: Pickthall
47.text: And He it is Who maketh night a covering for you, and sleep repose, and maketh day a resurrection.

47.author: Qarai
47.text: It is He who made for you the night as a covering and sleep for rest and He made the day a recall to life.

47.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
47.text: It is He who has appointed the night a mantle for you and sleep for a rest. The day He has appointed for rising.

47.author: Saheeh International
47.text: And it is He who has made the night for you as clothing and sleep [a means for] rest and has made the day a resurrection.

47.author: Sarwar
47.text: It is He who has made the night as a covering for you, sleep as a rest for you, and the day for you to rise again.

47.author: Shakir
47.text: And He it is Who made the night a covering for you, and the sleep a rest, and He made the day to rise up again.

47.author: Wahiduddin Khan
47.text: It is He who made the night a mantle for you, and sleep for repose; and made the day a time for rising.

47.author: Yusuf Ali
47.text: And He it is Who makes the Night as a Robe for you, and Sleep as Repose, and makes the Day (as it were) a Resurrection.

47.author: Transliteration
47.text: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala lakumu allayla libasan waalnnawma subatan wajaAAala alnnahara nushooran

48.author: Ahmed Ali
48.text: It is He who sends the winds with auspicious news in advance of His benevolence; and We send pure water down from the sky

48.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
48.text: And it is He Who sent the winds giving glad tidings before His mercy; and We sent down purifying water from the sky.

48.author: Arberry
48.text: And it is He who has loosed the winds, bearing good tidings before His mercy; and We sent down from heaven pure water

48.author: Daryabadi
48.text: And it is He who sendeth fort the winds as a herald before His mercy and We send down from the heaven water pure.

48.author: Hilali & Khan
48.text: And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His Mercy (rain), and We send down pure water from the sky,

48.author: Itani
48.text: And it is He who sends the winds, bringing advance news of His mercy; and We send down from the sky pure water.

48.author: Maududi
48.text: And it is He, Who drives the winds to be the harbingers of His mercy: then He sends down pure water from the sky

48.author: Mubarakpuri
48.text: And it is He Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His mercy; and We send down pure water from the sky,

48.author: Pickthall
48.text: And He it is Who sendeth the winds, glad tidings heralding His mercy, and We send down purifying water from the sky,

48.author: Qarai
48.text: And it is He who sends the winds as harbingers of His mercy, and We send down from the sky purifying water,

48.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
48.text: It is He who loosens the winds, bearing glad tidings before the Hands of His Mercy, and We have sent down pure water from the heaven,

48.author: Saheeh International
48.text: And it is He who sends the winds as good tidings before His mercy, and We send down from the sky pure water

48.author: Sarwar
48.text: It is He who sends the winds to you with the glad news of His mercy and who sends purifying rain from the sky

48.author: Shakir
48.text: And He it is Who sends the winds as good news before His mercy; and We send down pure water from the cloud,

48.author: Wahiduddin Khan
48.text: It is He who sends the winds as heralds of His mercy and We send down pure water from the sky,

48.author: Yusuf Ali
48.text: And He it is Who sends the winds as heralds of glad tidings, going before His mercy, and We send down pure water from the sky,-

48.author: Transliteration
48.text: Wahuwa allathee arsala alrriyaha bushran bayna yaday rahmatihi waanzalna mina alssamai maan tahooran

49.author: Ahmed Ali
49.text: To quicken a region that was dead, and to give it as drink to animals We have created and to men in plenty.

49.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
49.text: In order that We may revive a dead city with it, and give it to the many beasts and men that We have created, to drink.

49.author: Arberry
49.text: so that We might revive a dead land, and give to drink of it, of that We created, cattle and men a many.

49.author: Daryabadi
49.text: That We may quicken thereby a dead land, and We give drink thereof to that which We have created of cattle and human beings many.

49.author: Hilali & Khan
49.text: That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give to drink thereof many of the cattle and men that We had created.

49.author: Itani
49.text: To revive dead lands thereby, and to provide drink for the multitude of animals and humans We created.

49.author: Maududi
49.text: so that He may revive the dead land, and quench the thirst of many of His creatures from among beasts and men.

49.author: Mubarakpuri
49.text: That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give to drink thereof many of the cattle and men that We have created.

49.author: Pickthall
49.text: That We may give life thereby to a dead land, and We give many beasts and men that We have created to drink thereof.

49.author: Qarai
49.text: with which We revive a dead country and provide water to many of the cattle and humans We have created.

49.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
49.text: so, that with it We revive dead lands and provide drink for the cattle and the human We created.

49.author: Saheeh International
49.text: That We may bring to life thereby a dead land and give it as drink to those We created of numerous livestock and men.

49.author: Sarwar
49.text: to revive the barren land and provide water for many creatures, cattle, and people.

49.author: Shakir
49.text: That We may give life thereby to a dead land and give it for drink, out of what We have created, to cattle and many people.

49.author: Wahiduddin Khan
49.text: so that We may bring life to a dead land; and slake the thirst of Our creation; cattle and men, in great numbers.

49.author: Yusuf Ali
49.text: That with it We may give life to a dead land, and slake the thirst of things We have created,- cattle and men in great numbers.

49.author: Transliteration
49.text: Linuhyiya bihi baldatan maytan wanusqiyahu mimma khalaqna anAAaman waanasiyya katheeran

50.author: Ahmed Ali
50.text: And We distribute it among them in various ways that they may ponder and reflect; yet most men disdain everything but denial and thanklessness.

50.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
50.text: And indeed We kept cycles of water among them that they may remember; so most men did not accept, except to be ungrateful.

50.author: Arberry
50.text: We have indeed turned it about amongst them, so that they may remember; yet most men refuse all but unbelief.

50.author: Daryabadi
50.text: And We set it forth among them, that they may be admonished, but most men begrudge aught save infidelity.

50.author: Hilali & Khan
50.text: And indeed We have distributed it (rain or water) amongst them in order that they may remember the Grace of Allah, but most men refuse (or deny the Truth or Faith) and accept nothing but disbelief or ingratitude.

50.author: Itani
50.text: We have circulated it among them, that they may reflect, but most people persist in thanklessness.

50.author: Maududi
50.text: We present the same phenomenon over and over again before them so that they may learn a lesson from it; but most people decline to adopt any other attitude than of disbelief and ingratitude.

50.author: Mubarakpuri
50.text: And indeed We have distributed it among them in order that they may remember the grace of Allah, but most men refuse (out of) gratitude.

50.author: Pickthall
50.text: And verily We have repeated it among them that they may remember, but most of mankind begrudge aught save ingratitude.

50.author: Qarai
50.text: Certainly We distribute it among them so that they may take admonition. But most people are only intent on ingratitude.

50.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
50.text: We have indeed turned it about them, so that they remember; yet most people refuse all except disbelief.

50.author: Saheeh International
50.text: And We have certainly distributed it among them that they might be reminded, but most of the people refuse except disbelief.

50.author: Sarwar
50.text: We send them rain from time to time so that they may take heed. Many people have responded, but ungratefully.

50.author: Shakir
50.text: And certainly We have repeated this to them that they may be mindful, but the greater number of men do not consent to aught except denying.

50.author: Wahiduddin Khan
50.text: We have explained it to them in diverse ways, so that they may take heed, but most persist in their ingratitude.

50.author: Yusuf Ali
50.text: And We have distributed the (water) amongst them, in order that they may celebrate (our) praises, but most men are averse (to aught) but (rank) ingratitude.

50.author: Transliteration
50.text: Walaqad sarrafnahu baynahum liyaththakkaroo faaba aktharu alnnasi illa kufooran

51.author: Ahmed Ali
51.text: Had We pleased We could have raised a warner in every town.

51.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
51.text: If We willed, We could have sent a Herald of Warning to every village.

51.author: Arberry
51.text: If We had willed, We would have raised up in every city a warner.

51.author: Daryabadi
51.text: And if We had willed, We would have raised up in each town a warner.

51.author: Hilali & Khan
51.text: And had We willed, We would have raised a warner in every town.

51.author: Itani
51.text: Had We willed, We could have sent to every town a warner.

51.author: Maududi
51.text: Had We willed, We would have raised up a separate Warner in each habitation.

51.author: Mubarakpuri
51.text: And had We willed, We would have raised a warner in every town.

51.author: Pickthall
51.text: If We willed, We could raise up a warner in every village.

51.author: Qarai
51.text: Had We wished, We would have sent a warner to every town.

51.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
51.text: Had it been Our will, We could have raised a warner in every village.

51.author: Saheeh International
51.text: And if We had willed, We could have sent into every city a warner.

51.author: Sarwar
51.text: Had We wanted We could have sent a Prophet to every town.

51.author: Shakir
51.text: And if We had pleased We would certainly have raised a warner in every town.

51.author: Wahiduddin Khan
51.text: If We had so wished, We might have sent a warner into every town,

51.author: Yusuf Ali
51.text: Had it been Our Will, We could have sent a warner to every centre of population.

51.author: Transliteration
51.text: Walaw shina labaAAathna fee kulli qaryatin natheeran

52.author: Ahmed Ali
52.text: So do not listen to unbelievers, and strive against them with greater effort.

52.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
52.text: So do not listen to the disbelievers and, with this Qur’an, fight a great war against them.

52.author: Arberry
52.text: So obey not the unbelievers, but struggle with them thereby mightily.

52.author: Daryabadi
52.text: Se obey not thou the infidels, but strive against them therewith with a great striving.

52.author: Hilali & Khan
52.text: So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them (by preaching) with the utmost endeavour, with it (the Quran).

52.author: Itani
52.text: So do not obey the disbelievers, but strive against them with it, a mighty struggle.

52.author: Maududi
52.text: So, O Prophet, do not yield to the disbelievers, but wage a Jihad against them with this Qur'an.

52.author: Mubarakpuri
52.text: So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them with the utmost endeavor with it.

52.author: Pickthall
52.text: So obey not the disbelievers, but strive against them herewith with a great endeavour.

52.author: Qarai
52.text: So do not obey the faithless, but wage a great jihad against them with it.

52.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
52.text: So do not obey the unbelievers, but struggle mightily with it (the Koran).

52.author: Saheeh International
52.text: So do not obey the disbelievers, and strive against them with the Qur'an a great striving.

52.author: Sarwar
52.text: Do not yield to the unbelievers but launch a great campaign against them with the help of the Quran.

52.author: Shakir
52.text: So do not follow the unbelievers, and strive against them a mighty striving with it.

52.author: Wahiduddin Khan
52.text: so do not yield to those who deny the truth, but strive with the utmost strenuousness by means of this [Quran, to convey its message to them].

52.author: Yusuf Ali
52.text: Therefore listen not to the Unbelievers, but strive against them with the utmost strenuousness, with the (Qur'an).

52.author: Transliteration
52.text: Fala tutiAAi alkafireena wajahidhum bihi jihadan kabeeran

53.author: Ahmed Ali
53.text: It is He who made two bodies of water flow side by side, one fresh (and) sweet, the other brine (and) bitter, and has placed an interstice, a barrier between them.

53.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
53.text: And it is He Who caused the two joint seas to flow- one is palatable, very sweet, and the other is salty, very bitter; and kept a veil between them and a preventing barrier.

53.author: Arberry
53.text: And it is He who let forth the two seas, this one Sweet, grateful to taste, and this salt, bitter to the tongue, and He set between them a barrier, and a ban forbidden.

53.author: Daryabadi
53.text: And it is He who hath mixed the two seas: this, sweet ond thirst quenching; that, saltish ond bitter; and hath placed between the twain a barrier and a great partition complete.

53.author: Hilali & Khan
53.text: And it is He Who has let free the two seas (kinds of water), one palatable and sweet, and the other salt and bitter, and He has set a barrier and a complete partition between them.

53.author: Itani
53.text: And it is He who merged the two seas; this one fresh and sweet, and that one salty and bitter; and He placed between them a barrier, and an impassable boundary.

53.author: Maududi
53.text: And it is He, Who has let loose the two seas, one palatable and sweet, the other bitter and saltish, and there is a partition between them, which is an insurmountable barrier.

53.author: Mubarakpuri
53.text: And it is He Who has let free the two seas, this is palatable and sweet, and that is salty and bitter; and He has set a barrier and a complete partition between them.

53.author: Pickthall
53.text: And He it is Who hath given independence to the two seas (though they meet); one palatable, sweet, and the other saltish, bitter; and hath set a bar and a forbidding ban between them.

53.author: Qarai
53.text: It is He who merged the two seas: this one sweet and agreeable, and that one briny and bitter, and between the two He set a barrier and a forbidding hindrance.

53.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
53.text: It was He who let forth the two seas, this one is palatably sweet and this salt, a bitter taste, and He set a barrier between them, and a refuge which is forbidden.

53.author: Saheeh International
53.text: And it is He who has released [simultaneously] the two seas, one fresh and sweet and one salty and bitter, and He placed between them a barrier and prohibiting partition.

53.author: Sarwar
53.text: It is He who has joined the two seas; one palatable and sweet, the other bitterly salty and has established a barrier between them as a partition.

53.author: Shakir
53.text: And He it is Who has made two seas to flow freely, the one sweet that subdues thirst by its sweetness, and the other salt that burns by its saltness; and between the two He has made a barrier and inviolable obstruction.

53.author: Wahiduddin Khan
53.text: It is He who released the two bodies of flowing water, one sweet and fresh and the other salty and bitter, and set up an insurmountable barrier between them.

53.author: Yusuf Ali
53.text: It is He Who has let free the two bodies of flowing water: One palatable and sweet, and the other salt and bitter; yet has He made a barrier between them, a partition that is forbidden to be passed.

53.author: Transliteration
53.text: Wahuwa allathee maraja albahrayni hatha AAathbun furatun wahatha milhun ojajun wajaAAala baynahuma barzakhan wahijran mahjooran

54.author: Ahmed Ali
54.text: It is He who created man from water, then gave him consanguinity and affinity. Your Lord is omnipotent.

54.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
54.text: And it is He Who created man from water, then appointed relatives and in-laws for him; and your Lord is All Able.

54.author: Arberry
54.text: And it is He who created of water a mortal, and made him kindred of blood and marriage; thy Lord is All-powerful.

54.author: Daryabadi
54.text: And it is He who hath created man from water, and then made for him kinship by blood and marriage. And thy Lord is ever potent.

54.author: Hilali & Khan
54.text: And it is He Who has created man from water, and has appointed for him kindred by blood, and kindred by marriage. And your Lord is Ever All-Powerful to do what He will.

54.author: Itani
54.text: And it is He who, from fluid, created the human being. Then He made relationships through marriage and mating. Your Lord is Omnipotent.

54.author: Maududi
54.text: And it is He, Who created man from water: then from him He caused two kinds of kindred, by blood and by marriage: your Lord is AI I-Powerful.

54.author: Mubarakpuri
54.text: And it is He Who has created man from water, and has appointed for him kindred by blood, and kindred by marriage. And your Lord is Ever All-Powerful to do what He wills.

54.author: Pickthall
54.text: And He it is Who hath created man from water, and hath appointed for him kindred by blood and kindred by marriage; for thy Lord is ever Powerful.

54.author: Qarai
54.text: It is He who created the human being from water, then invested him with ties of blood and marriage, and your Lord is all-powerful.

54.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
54.text: And it is He who created the human from water and gave him kindred of blood and of marriage. Your Lord is the Powerful.

54.author: Saheeh International
54.text: And it is He who has created from water a human being and made him [a relative by] lineage and marriage. And ever is your Lord competent [concerning creation].

54.author: Sarwar
54.text: It is He who has created the human being from water to have relationships of both lineage and wedlock. Your Lord has all power.

54.author: Shakir
54.text: And He it is Who has created man from the water, then He has made for him blood relationship and marriage relationship, and your Lord is powerful.

54.author: Wahiduddin Khan
54.text: It is He who has created human beings from water and He has granted them the ties of blood as well as marriage. Your Lord is all powerful.

54.author: Yusuf Ali
54.text: It is He Who has created man from water: then has He established relationships of lineage and marriage: for thy Lord has power (over all things).

54.author: Transliteration
54.text: Wahuwa allathee khalaqa mina almai basharan fajaAAalahu nasaban wasihran wakana rabbuka qadeeran

55.author: Ahmed Ali
55.text: And yet they worship besides God what cannot bring them gain or do them harm. The unbeliever has always been an auxiliary against his Lord.

55.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
55.text: And they worship such, other than Allah, which neither benefit nor hurt them; and the disbeliever helps the devil instead of his Lord.

55.author: Arberry
55.text: And they serve, apart from God, what neither profits them nor hurts them; and the unbeliever is ever a partisan against his Lord.

55.author: Daryabadi
55.text: And yet they worship, besides Allah, that which can neither benefit them nor hurt them; and the infidel is ever an aider of the devil against his Lord.

55.author: Hilali & Khan
55.text: And they (disbelievers, polytheists, etc.) worship besides Allah, that which can neither profit them nor harm them, and the disbeliever is ever a helper (of the Satan) against his Lord.

55.author: Itani
55.text: And yet, instead of God, they serve what neither profits them nor harms them. The disbeliever has always turned his back on his Lord.

55.author: Maududi
55.text: Yet they worship, instead of Allah, those who can neither benefit them nor harm them: more than this, the disbeliever has become a helper of every rebel against his Lord.

55.author: Mubarakpuri
55.text: And they worship besides Allah, that which can neither profit them nor harm them; and the disbeliever is ever a helper against his Lord.

55.author: Pickthall
55.text: Yet they worship instead of Allah that which can neither benefit them nor hurt them. The disbeliever was ever a partisan against his Lord.

55.author: Qarai
55.text: They worship besides Allah that which neither brings them any benefit nor causes them any harm, and the faithless one is ever an abettor against his Lord.

55.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
55.text: Yet they (the unbelievers) worship, other than Allah, that which can neither benefit nor harm them. Surely, the unbeliever is ever a partisan against his Lord.

55.author: Saheeh International
55.text: But they worship rather than Allah that which does not benefit them or harm them, and the disbeliever is ever, against his Lord, an assistant [to Satan].

55.author: Sarwar
55.text: They worship besides God things that can neither benefit nor harm them. The unbelievers are defiant against their Lord.

55.author: Shakir
55.text: And they serve besides Allah that which neither profits them nor causes them harm; and the unbeliever is a partisan against his Lord.

55.author: Wahiduddin Khan
55.text: Yet they worship besides God that which can neither benefit them nor harm them. One who denies the truth is a helper [of evil] against his Lord.

55.author: Yusuf Ali
55.text: Yet do they worship, besides Allah, things that can neither profit them nor harm them: and the Misbeliever is a helper (of Evil), against his own Lord!

55.author: Transliteration
55.text: WayaAAbudoona min dooni Allahi ma la yanfaAAuhum wala yadurruhum wakana alkafiru AAala rabbihi thaheeran

56.author: Ahmed Ali
56.text: Yet We have not sent you but to give good tidings and to warn.

56.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
56.text: And We have not sent you (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) except as a Herald of glad tidings and warnings.

56.author: Arberry
56.text: We have sent thee not, except good tidings to bear, and warning.

56.author: Daryabadi
56.text: And We sent thee but as a bearer of glad tidings and warner.

56.author: Hilali & Khan
56.text: And We have sent you (O Muhammad SAW) only as a bearer of glad tidings and a warner.

56.author: Itani
56.text: We sent you only as a herald of good news and a warner.

56.author: Maududi
56.text: O Muhammad, We have sent you only to proclaim good tidings and to give warning.

56.author: Mubarakpuri
56.text: And We have sent you only as a bearer of good news and a warner.

56.author: Pickthall
56.text: And We have sent thee (O Muhammad) only as a bearer of good tidings and a warner.

56.author: Qarai
56.text: We did not send you except as a bearer of good news and as a warner.

56.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
56.text: We did not send you but as a bearer of glad tidings and as a warner.

56.author: Saheeh International
56.text: And We have not sent you, [O Muhammad], except as a bringer of good tidings and a warner.

56.author: Sarwar
56.text: We have sent you for no other reason but to be a bearer of glad news and warning.

56.author: Shakir
56.text: And We have not sent you but as a giver of good news and as a warner.

56.author: Wahiduddin Khan
56.text: We have sent you only as a bearer of glad tidings and as a warner.

56.author: Yusuf Ali
56.text: But thee We only sent to give glad tidings and admonition.

57.author: Ahmed Ali
57.text: Tell them: "I do not ask any recompense of you for this other than (urging) whoever likes may take the way to his Lord."

57.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
57.text: Proclaim, “I do not ask any fee from you for this, except that whoever wills may take the way to his Lord.”

57.author: Arberry
57.text: Say: 'I do not ask of you a wage for this, except for him who wishes to take to his Lord a way.'

57.author: Daryabadi
57.text: Say thou. ask of you no hire for this, save that whosoever will may take unto his Lord a way.

57.author: Hilali & Khan
57.text: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this (that which I have brought from my Lord and its preaching, etc.), save that whosoever wills, may take a Path to his Lord.

57.author: Itani
57.text: Say, “I ask of you no payment for this—only that whoever wills may take a path to his Lord.”

57.author: Maududi
57.text: Tell them, "I do not ask of you any recompense for this work: I only ask of the one, who will, to adopt the way of his Lord.

57.author: Mubarakpuri
57.text: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for this, save that whosoever wills may take a path to his Lord."

57.author: Pickthall
57.text: Say: I ask of you no reward for this, save that whoso will may choose a way unto his Lord.

57.author: Qarai
57.text: Say, ‘I do not ask you any reward for it, except that anyone who wishes should take the way to his Lord.’

57.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
57.text: Say: 'I demand of you no wage for this except for he who wishes to take the Path to his Lord'

57.author: Saheeh International
57.text: Say, "I do not ask of you for it any payment - only that whoever wills might take to his Lord a way."

57.author: Sarwar
57.text: Tell them, "I ask no recompense for my preaching to you, except the fact that whoever wants should choose the way of God."

57.author: Shakir
57.text: Say: I do not ask you aught in return except that he who will, may take the way to his Lord.

57.author: Wahiduddin Khan
57.text: Say, "I do not ask you for any recompense for this except that anyone who so wishes should take the right path to his Lord."

57.author: Yusuf Ali
57.text: Say: "No reward do I ask of you for it but this: that each one who will may take a (straight) Path to his Lord."

57.author: Transliteration
57.text: Qul ma asalukum AAalayhi min ajrin illa man shaa an yattakhitha ila rabbihi sabeelan

58.author: Ahmed Ali
58.text: Have trust in God the Living, who will never die, and sing His hallelujas; for He is well aware of the sins of His creatures.

58.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
58.text: And trust the Living One Who will never die, and praising Him proclaim His Purity; and He is Sufficient upon the sins of His bondmen, All Aware.

58.author: Arberry
58.text: Put thy trust in the Living God, the Undying, and proclaim His praise. Sufficiently is He aware of His servants sins

58.author: Daryabadi
58.text: And trust thou in the Living One who dieth not, and hallow His praise; it sufficeth that he of the sins of His bondmen is Aware.

58.author: Hilali & Khan
58.text: And put your trust (O Muhammad SAW) in the Ever Living One Who dies not, and glorify His Praises, and Sufficient is He as the All-Knower of the sins of His slaves;

58.author: Itani
58.text: And put your trust in the Living, the One who never dies; and celebrate His praise. He suffices as the All-Informed Knower of the faults of His creatures.

58.author: Maududi
58.text: O Muhammad trust in that Allah Who is Ever-Living and will never die' Glorify Him with His praise, for He alone is sufficient to be aware of the sins of His servants.

58.author: Mubarakpuri
58.text: And put your trust in the Ever Living One Who dies not, and glorify His praises, and sufficient is He as the All-Knower of the sins of His servants,

58.author: Pickthall
58.text: And trust thou in the Living One Who dieth not, and hymn His praise. He sufficeth as the Knower of His bondmen's sins,

58.author: Qarai
58.text: Put your trust in the Living One who does not die, and celebrate His praise. He suffices as one all-aware of the sins of His servants.

58.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
58.text: Put your trust in the All Living who never dies, and exalt with His praise, He is sufficiently aware of His worshipers' sins.

58.author: Saheeh International
58.text: And rely upon the Ever-Living who does not die, and exalt [Allah] with His praise. And sufficient is He to be, with the sins of His servants, Acquainted -

58.author: Sarwar
58.text: Also trust in the Living One who never dies and glorify Him with His praise. He has sufficient knowledge of the sins of His servants.

58.author: Shakir
58.text: And rely on the Ever-living Who dies not, and celebrate His praise; and Sufficient is He as being aware of the faults of His servants,

58.author: Wahiduddin Khan
58.text: Put your trust in the One who is the Ever-Living [God], who never dies, and glorify Him with His praise. He is fully aware of the sins of His servants;

58.author: Yusuf Ali
58.text: And put thy trust in Him Who lives and dies not; and celebrate his praise; and enough is He to be acquainted with the faults of His servants;-

58.author: Transliteration
58.text: Watawakkal AAala alhayyi allathee la yamootu wasabbih bihamdihi wakafa bihi bithunoobi AAibadihi khabeeran

59.author: Ahmed Ali
59.text: He created the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them in six spans then assumed His authority. He is the benevolent: Ask those who are well-informed.

59.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
59.text: The One Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six days, then befitting His Majesty, established Himself upon the Throne (of control); the Most Gracious – therefore ask the one who knows, concerning Him!

59.author: Arberry
