59.text: who created the heavens and the earth, and what between them is, in six days, then sat Himself upon the Throne, the All-compassionate: ask any informed of Him!

59.author: Daryabadi
59.text: Who created the heavens and the earth and whatsoever is in- between them in six days, then He established Himself on the Throne -the Compassionate! so, concernin Him, ask any one informed.

59.author: Hilali & Khan
59.text: Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days. Then He Istawa (rose over) the Throne (in a manner that suits His Majesty). The Most Beneficent (Allah)! Ask Him (O Prophet Muhammad SAW), (concerning His Qualities, His rising over His Throne, His creations, etc.), as He is Al-Khabir (The All-Knower of everything i.e. Allah).

59.author: Itani
59.text: He who created the heavens and the earth and everything between them in six days, then settled on the Throne. The Most Merciful. Ask about Him a well-informed.

59.author: Maududi
59.text: In six days He created the earth and the heavens and all that is between them; then He established Himself on the "Throne" (of the Kingdom of the uriverse). (He is) the Merciful: as to His Glory, ask the one who knows.

59.author: Mubarakpuri
59.text: Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days. Then He rose over (Istawa) the Throne. The Most Gracious! Ask Him, as He is the All-Knower.

59.author: Pickthall
59.text: Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days, then He mounted the Throne. The Beneficent! Ask anyone informed concerning Him!

59.author: Qarai
59.text: He, who created the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them in six days, and then settled on the Throne, the All-beneficent; so ask someone who is well aware about Him.

59.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
59.text: (It is) He who, in six days created the heavens and the earth and all that lies between them, and then He willed to the Throne. The Merciful; ask about Him from he who knows Him.

59.author: Saheeh International
59.text: He who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six days and then established Himself above the Throne - the Most Merciful, so ask about Him one well informed.

59.author: Sarwar
59.text: It is He who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six days and then He established His domination over the Throne. He is the Beneficent God. Refer to Him as the final authority.

59.author: Shakir
59.text: Who created the heavens and the earth and what is between them in six periods, and He is firmly established on the throne of authority; the Beneficent Allah, so ask respecting it one aware.

59.author: Wahiduddin Khan
59.text: it is He who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between them in six Days [periods], then settled Himself on the throne -- the Gracious One. Ask any informed person about Him.

59.author: Yusuf Ali
59.text: He Who created the heavens and the earth and all that is between, in six days, and is firmly established on the Throne (of Authority): Allah Most Gracious: ask thou, then, about Him of any acquainted (with such things).

59.author: Transliteration
59.text: Allathee khalaqa alssamawati waalarda wama baynahuma fee sittati ayyamin thumma istawa AAala alAAarshi alrrahmanu faisal bihi khabeeran

60.author: Ahmed Ali
60.text: When you say to them: "Bow before Ar-Rahman," they say: "What is Ar-Rahman? Should we adore whoever you ask us to?" And their aversion increases further.

60.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
60.text: And when it is said to them, “Prostrate to the Most Gracious” – they say, “And what is the Most Gracious? Shall we prostrate to whatever you command us?” And this command only increases the hatred in them. (Command of Prostration # 7)

60.author: Arberry
60.text: But when they are told, 'Bow yourselves to the All-merciful,' they say, 'And what is the All-merciful? Shall we bow ourselves to what thou biddest us?' And it increases them in aversion.

60.author: Daryabadi
60.text: And when it is said unto them: prostrate yourselves unto the Compassionate, they say: and what is the Compassionate? Shall we prostrate ourselves unto that which thou commandest us? And it increaseth in them aversion.

60.author: Hilali & Khan
60.text: And when it is said to them: "Prostrate to the Most Beneficent (Allah)! They say: "And what is the Most Beneficent? Shall we fall down in prostration to that which you (O Muhammad SAW) command us?" And it increases in them only aversion.

60.author: Itani
60.text: And when it is said to them, “Bow down to the Merciful,” they say, “And what is the Merciful? Are we to bow down to whatever you command us?” And it increases their aversion.

60.author: Maududi
60.text: When it is said to them. "Prostrate yourselves before the Merciful," they retort, "What is the Merciful? Would you have us prostrate ourselves before whomsoever you will?" And this invitation only helps to increase their hatred all the more.

60.author: Mubarakpuri
60.text: And when it is said to them: "Prostrate yourselves to Ar-Rahman!" They say: "And what is Ar-Rahman Shall we fall down in prostration to that which you command us" And it increases in them only aversion.

60.author: Pickthall
60.text: And when it is said unto them: Prostrate to the Beneficent! they say: And what is the Beneficent? Are we to prostrate to whatever thou (Muhammad) biddest us? And it increaseth aversion in them.

60.author: Qarai
60.text: When they are told: ‘Prostrate yourselves before the All-beneficent,’ they say, ‘What is ‘‘the All-beneficent’’? Shall we prostrate ourselves before whatever you bid us?’ And it increases their aversion.

60.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
60.text: When it is said to them: 'Prostrate yourselves before the Merciful', they ask: 'And what is the Merciful? Shall we prostrate ourselves to whatever you bid us' And it increases their aversion.

60.author: Saheeh International
60.text: And when it is said to them, "Prostrate to the Most Merciful," they say, "And what is the Most Merciful? Should we prostrate to that which you order us?" And it increases them in aversion.

60.author: Sarwar
60.text: When they are told to prostrate themselves before the Beneficent God, they say, "Who is the Beneficent God? Why should we prostrate ourselves before the one whom you have commanded us to?" This only increases their rebelliousness.

60.author: Shakir
60.text: And when it is said to them: Prostrate to the Beneficent Allah, they say: And what is the Allah of beneficence? Shall we prostrate to what you bid us? And it adds to their aversion.

60.author: Wahiduddin Khan
60.text: When they are told, "Prostrate yourselves before the Gracious One," they ask, "Who is this Gracious One? Shall we prostrate ourselves before whatever you will?" This increases their aversion.

60.author: Yusuf Ali
60.text: When it is said to them, "Prostrate to (Allah) Most Gracious!", they say, "And what is (Allah) Most Gracious? Shall we prostrate to that which thou commandest us?" And it increases their flight (from the Truth).

60.author: Transliteration
60.text: Waitha qeela lahumu osjudoo lilrrahmani qaloo wama alrrahmanu anasjudu lima tamuruna wazadahum nufooran

61.author: Ahmed Ali
61.text: Blessed is He who placed in the heavens constellations of stars, and placed a burning lamp in it and the luminous moon.

61.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
61.text: Most Auspicious is He Who created lofty towers in the sky and placed a lamp in it, and the luminous moon.

61.author: Arberry
61.text: Blessed be He who has set in heaven constellations, and has set among them a lamp, and an illuminating moon.

61.author: Daryabadi
61.text: Blest be He Who hath placed big stars in the heaven, and hath placed therein a lamp and a moon enlightening.

61.author: Hilali & Khan
61.text: Blessed be He Who has placed in the heaven big stars, and has placed therein a great lamp (sun), and a moon giving light.

61.author: Itani
61.text: Blessed is He who placed constellations in the sky, and placed in it a lamp, and an illuminating moon.

61.author: Maududi
61.text: Highly blessed is He, Who has made fortified spheres in the heavens and has set in it a "lamp" and a shining moon.

61.author: Mubarakpuri
61.text: Blessed be He Who has placed in the heaven Al-Buruj, and has placed therein a great lamp, and a moon giving light.

61.author: Pickthall
61.text: Blessed be He Who hath placed in the heaven mansions of the stars, and hath placed therein a great lamp and a moon giving light!

61.author: Qarai
61.text: Blessed is He who appointed houses in the sky and set in it a lamp and a shining moon.

61.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
61.text: Blessed be He who has set the constellations in the heaven, and set amongst them a sun, and an illuminating moon.

61.author: Saheeh International
61.text: Blessed is He who has placed in the sky great stars and placed therein a [burning] lamp and luminous moon.

61.author: Sarwar
61.text: Blessed is He who has established constellations in the sky and made therein a lamp and a shining moon.

61.author: Shakir
61.text: Blessed is He Who made the constellations in the heavens and made therein a lamp and a shining moon.

61.author: Wahiduddin Khan
61.text: Exalted is He who put constellations in the heavens, a radiant lamp and an illuminating moon --

61.author: Yusuf Ali
61.text: Blessed is He Who made constellations in the skies, and placed therein a Lamp and a Moon giving light;

61.author: Transliteration
61.text: Tabaraka allathee jaAAala fee alssamai buroojan wajaAAala feeha sirajan waqamaran muneeran

62.author: Ahmed Ali
62.text: It is He who made the night and day an alternation for him who cares to reflect and be grateful.

62.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
62.text: And it is He Who created the alternation of the night and day for one who wishes to remember or intends to give thanks.

62.author: Arberry
62.text: And it is He who made the night and day a succession for whom He desires to remember or He desires to be thankful.

62.author: Daryabadi
62.text: And it is He Who hath appointed the night and the day a succession, for him who desireth to consider or desireth to be grateful.

62.author: Hilali & Khan
62.text: And He it is Who has put the night and the day in succession, for such who desires to remember or desires to show his gratitude.

62.author: Itani
62.text: And it is He who made the night and the day alternate—for whoever desires to reflect, or desires to show gratitude.

62.author: Maududi
62.text: He it is Who caused the night and the day to succeed each other so that everyone who desires may learn a lesson or become grateful.

62.author: Mubarakpuri
62.text: And He it is Who has put the night and the day in succession (Khilfatan), for such who desires to remember or desires to show his gratitude.

62.author: Pickthall
62.text: And He it is Who hath appointed night and day in succession, for him who desireth to remember, or desireth thankfulness.

62.author: Qarai
62.text: It is He who made the night and the day alternate for someone who desires to take admonition, or desires to give thanks.

62.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
62.text: It is He who has made the night and day follow each other for those whom He desires to remember or He desires to be thankful.

62.author: Saheeh International
62.text: And it is He who has made the night and the day in succession for whoever desires to remember or desires gratitude.

62.author: Sarwar
62.text: It is He who has made the night and the day, one proceeding the other, for whoever wants to take heed or give thanks.

62.author: Shakir
62.text: And He it is Who made the night and the day to follow each other for him who desires to be mindful or desires to be thankful.

62.author: Wahiduddin Khan
62.text: it is He who has made night and day succeed each other, a sign for those who would take heed and would be grateful.

62.author: Yusuf Ali
62.text: And it is He Who made the Night and the Day to follow each other: for such as have the will to celebrate His praises or to show their gratitude.

62.author: Transliteration
62.text: Wahuwa allathee jaAAala allayla waalnnahara khilfatan liman arada an yaththakkara aw arada shukooran

63.author: Ahmed Ali
63.text: Devotees of Ar-Rahman are those who walk with humility on the earth, and when they are addressed by the ignorant, say: 'Peace;"

63.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
63.text: And the bondmen of the Most Gracious who walk upon the earth humbly, and when the ignorant address them they answer, “Peace”. (Good –bye)

63.author: Arberry
63.text: The servants of the All-merciful are those who walk in the earth modestly and who, when the ignorant address them, say, 'Peace';

63.author: Daryabadi
63.text: And the bondmen of the Compassionate are those Who Walk upon the earth meekly and when the ignorant address them, they say peace

63.author: Hilali & Khan
63.text: And the slaves of the Most Beneficent (Allah) are those who walk on the earth in humility and sedateness, and when the foolish address them (with bad words) they reply back with mild words of gentleness.

63.author: Itani
63.text: The servants of the Merciful are those who walk the earth in humility, and when the ignorant address them, they say, “Peace.”

63.author: Maududi
63.text: The (true) servants of the Merciful are those who walk humbly on the earth who, when the ignorant people behave insolently towards them, say, "Peace to you"

63.author: Mubarakpuri
63.text: And the servants of the Most Gracious are those who walk on the earth Hawna, and when the foolish address them they say; "Salama."

63.author: Pickthall
63.text: The (faithful) slaves of the Beneficent are they who walk upon the earth modestly, and when the foolish ones address them answer: Peace;

63.author: Qarai
63.text: The servants of the All-beneficent are those who walk humbly on the earth, and when the ignorant address them, say, ‘Peace!’

63.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
63.text: The worshipers of the Merciful are those who walk humbly on the earth, and when the ignorant address them say: 'Peace'

63.author: Saheeh International
63.text: And the servants of the Most Merciful are those who walk upon the earth easily, and when the ignorant address them [harshly], they say [words of] peace,

63.author: Sarwar
63.text: (Among) the servants of the Beneficent God are those who walk gently on the earth and when addressed by the ignorant ones, their only response is, "Peace be with you."

63.author: Shakir
63.text: And the servants of the Beneficent Allah are they who walk on the earth in humbleness, and when the ignorant address them, they say: Peace.

63.author: Wahiduddin Khan
63.text: The true servants of the Gracious One are those who walk upon the earth with humility and when they are addressed by the ignorant ones, their response is, "Peace";

63.author: Yusuf Ali
63.text: And the servants of (Allah) Most Gracious are those who walk on the earth in humility, and when the ignorant address them, they say, "Peace!";

63.author: Transliteration
63.text: WaAAibadu alrrahmani allatheena yamshoona AAala alardi hawnan waitha khatabahumu aljahiloona qaloo salaman

64.author: Ahmed Ali
64.text: And those who spend their nights bowed and standing before their Lord;

64.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
64.text: And who spend the night prostrating and standing, for their Lord.

64.author: Arberry
64.text: who pass the night prostrate to their Lord and standing;

64.author: Daryabadi
64.text: And these who pass the night before their Lord, prostrate and standing up.

64.author: Hilali & Khan
64.text: And those who spend the night before their Lord, prostrate and standing.

64.author: Itani
64.text: And those who pass the night prostrating themselves to their Lord and standing up.

64.author: Maududi
64.text: who pass their nights in prostrating themselves and standing before their Lord:

64.author: Mubarakpuri
64.text: And those who spend the night in worship of their Lord, prostrate and standing.

64.author: Pickthall
64.text: And who spend the night before their Lord, prostrate and standing,

64.author: Qarai
64.text: Those who spend the night for their Lord, prostrating and standing [in worship].

64.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
64.text: who pass the night prostrating and standing to their Lord.

64.author: Saheeh International
64.text: And those who spend [part of] the night to their Lord prostrating and standing [in prayer]

64.author: Sarwar
64.text: They are those who spend the night worshipping their Lord, prostrating, and standing,

64.author: Shakir
64.text: And they who pass the night prostrating themselves before their Lord and standing.

64.author: Wahiduddin Khan
64.text: and those who spend the night prostrating themselves, and standing before their Lord,

64.author: Yusuf Ali
64.text: Those who spend the night in adoration of their Lord prostrate and standing;

65.author: Ahmed Ali
65.text: Who say: "O our Lord, avert from us the torment of Hell: Its punishment is surely continuous.

65.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
65.text: And who submit, “Our Lord – avert the punishment of hell from us; indeed its punishment is a permanent neck-shackle.”

65.author: Arberry
65.text: who say, 'Our Lord, turn Thou from us the chastisement of Gehenna; surely its chastisement is torment most terrible;

65.author: Daryabadi
65.text: And these who say: our Lord! avert from us the torment of Hell verily the torment thereof is perishment.

65.author: Hilali & Khan
65.text: And those who say: "Our Lord! Avert from us the torment of Hell. Verily! Its torment is ever an inseparable, permanent punishment."

65.author: Itani
65.text: And those who say, ‘‘Our Lord, avert from us the suffering of Hell, for its suffering is continuous.

65.author: Maududi
65.text: who pray, "Our Lord, save us from the torment of Hell, for its torment is killing:

65.author: Mubarakpuri
65.text: And those who say: "Our Lord! Avert from us the torment of Hell. Verily, its torment is ever an inseparable, permanent punishment."

65.author: Pickthall
65.text: And who say: Our Lord! Avert from us the doom of hell; lo! the doom thereof is anguish;

65.author: Qarai
65.text: Those who say, ‘Our Lord! Turn away from us the punishment of hell. Indeed its punishment is enduring.

65.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
65.text: Who say: 'Our Lord, turn from us the punishment of Gehenna, for its punishment is the most terrible;

65.author: Saheeh International
65.text: And those who say, "Our Lord, avert from us the punishment of Hell. Indeed, its punishment is ever adhering;

65.author: Sarwar
65.text: who pray, "Lord, protect us from the torment of hell; it is a great loss.

65.author: Shakir
65.text: And they who say: O our Lord! turn away from us the punishment of hell, surely the punishment thereof is a lasting

65.author: Wahiduddin Khan
65.text: who say, "Our Lord, ward off from us the punishment of Hell, for its punishment is a dreadful torment to suffer.

65.author: Yusuf Ali
65.text: Those who say, "Our Lord! avert from us the Wrath of Hell, for its Wrath is indeed an affliction grievous,-

65.author: Transliteration
65.text: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana isrif AAanna AAathaba jahannama inna AAathabaha kana gharaman

66.author: Ahmed Ali
66.text: It is indeed an evil halt and an evil abode;"

66.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
66.text: Indeed it is a most wretched abode.

66.author: Arberry
66.text: evil it is as a lodging-place and an abode';

66.author: Daryabadi
66.text: Verily ill it is as an abode and as a station.

66.author: Hilali & Khan
66.text: Evil indeed it (Hell) is as an abode and as a place to dwell.

66.author: Itani
66.text: It is indeed a miserable residence and destination.”

66.author: Maududi
66.text: it is an evil abode and an evil resting place":

66.author: Mubarakpuri
66.text: Evil indeed it is as an abode and as a place to rest in.

66.author: Pickthall
66.text: Lo! it is wretched as abode and station;

66.author: Qarai
66.text: Indeed it is an evil station and abode.’

66.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
66.text: it is an evil settling, and an evil residence'

66.author: Saheeh International
66.text: Indeed, it is evil as a settlement and residence."

66.author: Sarwar
66.text: It is a terrible abode and an evil station,"

66.author: Shakir
66.text: Surely it is an evil abode and (evil) place to stay.

66.author: Wahiduddin Khan
66.text: Indeed, it is an evil abode and evil dwelling-place."

66.author: Yusuf Ali
66.text: "Evil indeed is it as an abode, and as a place to rest in";

67.author: Ahmed Ali
67.text: Who are neither prodigal nor miserly in their spending but follow a middle path;

67.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
67.text: And those who, when spending, neither exceed the limits nor act miserly, and stay in moderation between the two.

67.author: Arberry
67.text: who, when they expend, are neither prodigal nor parsimonious, but between that is a just stand;

67.author: Daryabadi
67.text: And those who when they expend, are neither extravagant nor sparing, and it is a medium in-between.

67.author: Hilali & Khan
67.text: And those, who, when they spend, are neither extravagant nor niggardly, but hold a medium (way) between those (extremes).

67.author: Itani
67.text: And those who, when they spend, are neither wasteful nor stingy, but choose a middle course between that.

67.author: Maududi
67.text: who, when they spend, are neither extravagant nor miserly but keep the golden mean between the two (extremes):

67.author: Mubarakpuri
67.text: And those who, when they spend, are neither extravagant nor stingy, but are in a just balance between them.

67.author: Pickthall
67.text: And those who, when they spend, are neither prodigal nor grudging; and there is ever a firm station between the two;

67.author: Qarai
67.text: Those who are neither wasteful nor tightfisted when spending, but balanced between these [two extremes].

67.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
67.text: who when they spend are neither wasteful nor miserly, between that is a just stand,

67.author: Saheeh International
67.text: And [they are] those who, when they spend, do so not excessively or sparingly but are ever, between that, [justly] moderate

67.author: Sarwar
67.text: who in their spending are neither extravagant nor stingy but maintain moderation,

67.author: Shakir
67.text: And they who when they spend, are neither extravagant nor parsimonious, and (keep) between these the just mean.

67.author: Wahiduddin Khan
67.text: They are those who are neither extravagant nor niggardly, but keep a balance between the two;

67.author: Yusuf Ali
67.text: Those who, when they spend, are not extravagant and not niggardly, but hold a just (balance) between those (extremes);

67.author: Transliteration
67.text: Waallatheena itha anfaqoo lam yusrifoo walam yaqturoo wakana bayna thalika qawaman

68.author: Ahmed Ali
68.text: Who do not invoke any god apart from God, who do not take a life which God has forbidden except for a cause that is just, and do not fornicate -- and any one who does so will be punished for the crime,

68.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
68.text: And those who do not worship any other deity along with Allah, and do not unjustly kill any living thing which Allah has forbidden, nor commit adultery; and whoever does this will receive punishment.

68.author: Arberry
68.text: who call not upon another god with God, nor slay the soul God has forbidden except by right, neither fornicate, for whosoever does that shall meet the price

68.author: Daryabadi
68.text: And those who call not unto anot her god along with Allah and slay not any soul which Allah hath forbidden, save in justification, and commit not fornication. And Whosoever shall do this, Shall incur the meed.

68.author: Hilali & Khan
68.text: And those who invoke not any other ilah (god) along with Allah, nor kill such life as Allah has forbidden, except for just cause, nor commit illegal sexual intercourse and whoever does this shall receive the punishment.

68.author: Itani
68.text: And those who do not implore besides God any other god, and do not kill the soul which God has made sacred—except in the pursuit of justice—and do not commit adultery. Whoever does that will face penalties.

68.author: Maududi
68.text: who do not invoke any god but Allah nor kill a soul, which Allah has forbidden, unjustly, nor commit adultery. -He who does this shall be punished for his sin,

68.author: Mubarakpuri
68.text: And those who invoke not any other god along with Allah, nor kill such person as Allah has forbidden, except for just cause, nor commit illegal sexual intercourse -- and whoever does this shall receive Athama.

68.author: Pickthall
68.text: And those who cry not unto any other god along with Allah, nor take the life which Allah hath forbidden save in (course of) justice, nor commit adultery - and whoso doeth this shall pay the penalty;

68.author: Qarai
68.text: Those who do not invoke another deity besides Allah, and do not kill a soul [whose life] Allah has made inviolable, except with due cause, and do not commit fornication. (Whoever does that shall encounter its retribution,

68.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
68.text: who do not call upon another god with Allah, nor slay the soul which Allah has forbidden except by right; who do not fornicate, for he who does this shall face punishment

68.author: Saheeh International
68.text: And those who do not invoke with Allah another deity or kill the soul which Allah has forbidden [to be killed], except by right, and do not commit unlawful sexual intercourse. And whoever should do that will meet a penalty.

68.author: Sarwar
68.text: who do not worship idols besides God, nor without a just cause murder a soul to whom God has granted amnesty, who do not commit fornication, for those who do so have committed a sin

68.author: Shakir
68.text: And they who do not call upon another god with Allah and do not slay the soul, which Allah has forbidden except in the requirements of justice, and (who) do not commit fornication and he who does this shall find a requital of sin;

68.author: Wahiduddin Khan
68.text: those who never invoke any other deity besides God, nor take a life which God has made sacred, except with the right to do so, nor commit adultery. Anyone who does that shall face punishment:

68.author: Yusuf Ali
68.text: Those who invoke not, with Allah, any other god, nor slay such life as Allah has made sacred except for just cause, nor commit fornication; - and any that does this (not only) meets punishment.

68.author: Transliteration
68.text: Waallatheena la yadAAoona maAAa Allahi ilahan akhara wala yaqtuloona alnnafsa allatee harrama Allahu illa bialhaqqi wala yaznoona waman yafAAal thalika yalqa athaman

69.author: Ahmed Ali
69.text: Whose punishment will be doubled on the Day of Judgement, and he will live for ever in disgrace,

69.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
69.text: The punishment shall be increased for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will remain in it forever, with humiliation.

69.author: Arberry
69.text: of sin-doubled shall be the chastisement for him on the Resurrection Day, and he shall dwell therein humbled,

69.author: Daryabadi
69.text: Multiplied for him shall be the torment on the Day of Resurrection, and he shall therein abide disgraced.

69.author: Hilali & Khan
69.text: The torment will be doubled to him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein in disgrace;

69.author: Itani
69.text: The punishment will be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will dwell therein in humiliation forever.

69.author: Maududi
69.text: and his torment shall be doubled on the Day of Resurrection, and he shall abide in a state of ignominy,

69.author: Mubarakpuri
69.text: The torment will be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein in disgrace;

69.author: Pickthall
69.text: The doom will be doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein disdained for ever;

69.author: Qarai
69.text: the punishment being doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection. In it he will abide in humiliation forever,

69.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
69.text: doubled for him on the Day of Resurrection is his punishment, and therein he shall live, humbled,

69.author: Saheeh International
69.text: Multiplied for him is the punishment on the Day of Resurrection, and he will abide therein humiliated -

69.author: Sarwar
69.text: and on the Day of Judgment their torment will be double. They will suffer forever in disgrace.

69.author: Shakir
69.text: The punishment shall be doubled to him on the day of resurrection, and he shall abide therein in abasement;

69.author: Wahiduddin Khan
69.text: he shall have his suffering doubled on the Day of Resurrection and he will abide forever in disgrace,

69.author: Yusuf Ali
69.text: (But) the Penalty on the Day of Judgment will be doubled to him, and he will dwell therein in ignominy,-

69.author: Transliteration
69.text: YudaAAaf lahu alAAathabu yawma alqiyamati wayakhlud feehi muhanan

70.author: Ahmed Ali
70.text: Except those who repent and come to believe and do the right, for whom God will turn evil into goodness, for God is forgiving and kind.

70.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
70.text: Except one who repents and accepts faith and does good deeds – so Allah will turn their evil deeds into virtues; and Allah is Oft Forgiving, Most Merciful.

70.author: Arberry
70.text: save him who repents, and believes, and does righteous work -- those, God will change their evil deeds into good deeds, for God is ever All-forgiving, All-compassionate;

70.author: Daryabadi
70.text: Save him who repenteth and believeth and worketh righteous work. Then these! for them Allah shall change their vices into virtues. Verily Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.

70.author: Hilali & Khan
70.text: Except those who repent and believe (in Islamic Monotheism), and do righteous deeds, for those, Allah will change their sins into good deeds, and Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

70.author: Itani
70.text: Except for those who repent, and believe, and do good deeds. These—God will replace their bad deeds with good deeds. God is ever Forgiving and Merciful.

70.author: Maududi
70.text: except the one who may have repented (after those sins) and have believed and done righteous works, for then Allah will change his evil deeds into good deeds, and He is very Forgiving and Merciful.

70.author: Mubarakpuri
70.text: Except those who repent and believe, and do righteous deeds; for those, Allah will change their sins into good deeds, and Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

70.author: Pickthall
70.text: Save him who repenteth and believeth and doth righteous work; as for such, Allah will change their evil deeds to good deeds. Allah is ever Forgiving, Merciful.

70.author: Qarai
70.text: except those who repent, attain faith, and act righteously. For such, Allah will replace their misdeeds with good deeds, and Allah is all-forgiving, all-merciful.

70.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
70.text: except he who repents and believes and does good works those, Allah will change their evil deeds into good deeds; Allah is ever Forgiving and Merciful.

70.author: Saheeh International
70.text: Except for those who repent, believe and do righteous work. For them Allah will replace their evil deeds with good. And ever is Allah Forgiving and Merciful.

70.author: Sarwar
70.text: But only those who repent and believe and act righteously will have their sins replaced by virtue; God is All-forgiving and All-merciful.

70.author: Shakir
70.text: Except him who repents and believes and does a good deed; so these are they of whom Allah changes the evil deeds to good ones; and Allah is Forgiving, Merciful.

70.author: Wahiduddin Khan
70.text: except for those who repent and believe and do good deeds. God will change the evil deeds of such people into good ones: He is most forgiving and most merciful.

70.author: Yusuf Ali
70.text: Unless he repents, believes, and works righteous deeds, for Allah will change the evil of such persons into good, and Allah is Oft-Forgiving, Most Merciful,

70.author: Transliteration
70.text: Illa man taba waamana waAAamila AAamalan salihan faolaika yubaddilu Allahu sayyiatihim hasanatin wakana Allahu ghafooran raheeman

71.author: Ahmed Ali
71.text: Whosoever repents and does the right, will have turned back to God by way of repentance;

71.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
71.text: And whoever repents and does good deeds – so he has inclined towards Allah with repentance as was required.

71.author: Arberry
71.text: and whosoever repents, and does righteousness, he truly turns to God in repentance.

71.author: Daryabadi
71.text: And whosoever repenteth and worketh righteously, then verily he repenteth toward Allah with a true repentance.

71.author: Hilali & Khan
71.text: And whosoever repents and does righteous good deeds, then verily, he repents towards Allah with true repentance.

71.author: Itani
71.text: Whoever repents and acts righteously—has inclined towards God with repentance.

71.author: Maududi
71.text: In fact, the one who repents and does righteous deeds, returns to Allah as one ightly should. -

71.author: Mubarakpuri
71.text: And whosoever repents and does righteous good deeds; then indeed he has repented to Allah Mataba.

71.author: Pickthall
71.text: And whosoever repenteth and doeth good, he verily repenteth toward Allah with true repentance -

71.author: Qarai
71.text: And whoever repents and acts righteously indeed turns to Allah with due penitence).

71.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
71.text: He who repents and does good works truly turns to Allah in repentance,

71.author: Saheeh International
71.text: And he who repents and does righteousness does indeed turn to Allah with [accepted] repentance.

71.author: Sarwar
71.text: Those who repent and act righteously have truly returned to God,

71.author: Shakir
71.text: And whoever repents and does good, he surely turns to Allah a (goodly) turning.

71.author: Wahiduddin Khan
71.text: He who repents and does good deeds has truly turned to God.

71.author: Yusuf Ali
71.text: And whoever repents and does good has truly turned to Allah with an (acceptable) conversion;-

71.author: Transliteration
71.text: Waman taba waAAamila salihan fainnahu yatoobu ila Allahi mataban

72.author: Ahmed Ali
72.text: And those who do not give false evidence, and if they come across unbecoming talk ignore it and pass by in a sedate way;

72.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
72.text: And those who do not give false testimony, and when they pass near some indecency, they pass by it saving their honour.

72.author: Arberry
72.text: And those who bear not false witness and, when they pass by idle talk, pass by with dignity;

72.author: Daryabadi
72.text: And those who witness not falsehood, and when they pass by some vanity pass by with dignity.

72.author: Hilali & Khan
72.text: And those who do not witness falsehood, and if they pass by some evil play or evil talk, they pass by it with dignity.

72.author: Itani
72.text: And those who do not bear false witness; and when they come across indecencies, they pass by with dignity.

72.author: Maududi
72.text: (And the servants of the Merciful are those:) who do not bear witness to falsehood and who; if they have ever to pass by what is vain, pass by like dignified people:

72.author: Mubarakpuri
72.text: And those who do not bear witness to falsehood, and if they pass by some evil play or evil talk, they pass by it with dignity.

72.author: Pickthall
72.text: And those who will not witness vanity, but when they pass near senseless play, pass by with dignity.

72.author: Qarai
72.text: Those who do not give false testimony, and when they come upon frivolity, pass by with dignity.

72.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
72.text: and those who do not bear false witness, and when they pass by idle talk, pass by with honor

72.author: Saheeh International
72.text: And [they are] those who do not testify to falsehood, and when they pass near ill speech, they pass by with dignity.

72.author: Sarwar
72.text: those who do not testify falsely and when they come across something impious, pass it by nobly,

72.author: Shakir
72.text: And they who do not bear witness to what is false, and when they pass by what is vain, they pass by nobly.

72.author: Wahiduddin Khan
72.text: And those who do not bear false witness, and when they pass by frivolity, they pass by with dignity;

72.author: Yusuf Ali
72.text: Those who witness no falsehood, and, if they pass by futility, they pass by it with honourable (avoidance);

72.author: Transliteration
72.text: Waallatheena la yashhadoona alzzoora waitha marroo biallaghwi marroo kiraman

73.author: Ahmed Ali
73.text: Who, when reminded of their Lord's revelations, do not fall for them like the deaf and blind;

73.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
73.text: And those who, when they are reminded of the signs of their Lord, do not fall deaf and blind upon them.

73.author: Arberry
73.text: who, when they are reminded of the signs of their Lord, fall not down thereat deaf and blind;

73.author: Daryabadi
73.text: And those who, when they are admonished by the commands of Allah, fall not down thereat, deaf and blind.

73.author: Hilali & Khan
73.text: And those who, when they are reminded of the Ayat (proofs, evidences, verses, lessons, signs, revelations, etc.) of their Lord, fall not deaf and blind thereat.

73.author: Itani
73.text: And those who, when reminded of the revelations of their Lord, do not fall before them deaf and blind.

73.author: Maududi
73.text: who do not behave like the blind and the deaf, when the Revelations of their Lord are recited to them for admonition;

73.author: Mubarakpuri
73.text: And those who, when they are reminded of the Ayat of their Lord, fall not deaf and blind thereat.

73.author: Pickthall
73.text: And those who, when they are reminded of the revelations of their Lord, fall not deaf and blind thereat.

73.author: Qarai
73.text: Those who, when reminded of the signs of their Lord, do not turn a deaf ear and a blind eye to them.

73.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
73.text: and who when they are reminded of the verses of their Lord, they do not fall down deaf and blind.

73.author: Saheeh International
73.text: And those who, when reminded of the verses of their Lord, do not fall upon them deaf and blind.

73.author: Sarwar
73.text: who, when reminded of the revelations of their Lord, do not try to ignore them as though deaf and blind. Rather, they try to understand and think about them.

73.author: Shakir
73.text: And they who, when reminded of the communications of their Lord, do not fall down thereat deaf and blind.

73.author: Wahiduddin Khan
73.text: who do not turn a blind eye and a deaf ear to the signs of their Lord when they are reminded of them;

73.author: Yusuf Ali
73.text: Those who, when they are admonished with the Signs of their Lord, droop not down at them as if they were deaf or blind;

73.author: Transliteration
73.text: Waallatheena itha thukkiroo biayati rabbihim lam yakhirroo AAalayha summan waAAumyanan

74.author: Ahmed Ali
74.text: And those who say: "O Lord, give us comfort in our spouses and children, and make us paragons of those who follow the straight path."

74.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
74.text: And who submit, “Our Lord, soothe our eyes with our wives and our children, and make us leaders of the pious.”

74.author: Arberry
74.text: who say, 'Our Lord, give us refreshment of our wives and seed, and make us a model to the godfearing.'

74.author: Daryabadi
74.text: And those who say: our Lord bestow on us coolness of eyes from our wives and our offspring, and make us unto the God-fearing a pattern.

74.author: Hilali & Khan
74.text: And those who say: "Our Lord! Bestow on us from our wives and our offspring who will be the comfort of our eyes, and make us leaders for the Muttaqun" (pious - see V. 2:2 and the footnote of V. 3:164)."

74.author: Itani
74.text: And those who say, “Our Lord, grant us delight in our spouses and our children, and make us a good example for the righteous.”

74.author: Maududi
74.text: who pray, "Our Lord, bless us with wives and children, who may be the comfort of our eyes, and make us leaders of the righteous. " -

74.author: Mubarakpuri
74.text: And those who say: "Our Lord! Bestow on us from our wives and our offspring the comfort of our eyes, and make us leaders of those who have Taqwa."

74.author: Pickthall
74.text: And who say: Our Lord! Vouchsafe us comfort of our wives and of our offspring, and make us patterns for (all) those who ward off (evil).

74.author: Qarai
74.text: And those who say, ‘Our Lord! Give us joy and comfort in our spouses and offspring, and make us imams of the Godwary.’

74.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
74.text: Those who say: 'Lord give us of our wives and children what pleases our eyes and make us leaders to the fearful'

74.author: Saheeh International
74.text: And those who say, "Our Lord, grant us from among our wives and offspring comfort to our eyes and make us an example for the righteous."

74.author: Sarwar
74.text: They pray, "Lord, let our spouses and children be the delight of our eyes and ourselves examples for the pious ones."

74.author: Shakir
74.text: And they who say: O our Lord! grant us in our wives and our offspring the joy of our eyes, and make us guides to those who guard (against evil).

74.author: Wahiduddin Khan
74.text: who say, "Lord, grant us joy in our wives and children and make us a model for the righteous."

74.author: Yusuf Ali
74.text: And those who pray, "Our Lord! Grant unto us wives and offspring who will be the comfort of our eyes, and give us (the grace) to lead the righteous."

74.author: Transliteration
74.text: Waallatheena yaqooloona rabbana hab lana min azwajina wathurriyyatina qurrata aAAyunin waijAAalna lilmuttaqeena imaman

75.author: Ahmed Ali
75.text: They will be rewarded for their perseverance with lofty mansions in empyrean where they will be received with greetings of peace and salutations,

75.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
75.text: They will be rewarded the highest position in heaven, the recompense of their patience, and they will be welcomed with honour and greetings of peace.

75.author: Arberry
75.text: Those shall be recompensed with the highest heaven, for that they endured patiently, and they shall receive therein a greeting and --' Peace!'

75.author: Daryabadi
75.text: Those shall be rewarded with the highest apartment, because they persevered. and therein they shall be met with a greeting and salutation-

75.author: Hilali & Khan
75.text: Those will be rewarded with the highest place (in Paradise) because of their patience. Therein they shall be met with greetings and the word of peace and respect.

75.author: Itani
75.text: Those will be awarded the Chamber for their patience, and will be greeted therein with greetings and peace.

75.author: Maududi
75.text: Such are the people who will be rewarded with high palaces for their fortitude,, wherein they will be welcomed with due respect, honour and salutations

75.author: Mubarakpuri
75.text: Those will be rewarded with the highest place because of their patience. Therein they shall be met with greetings and the word of peace and respect.

75.author: Pickthall
75.text: They will be awarded the high place forasmuch as they were steadfast, and they will meet therein with welcome and the ward of peace,

75.author: Qarai
75.text: Those shall be rewarded with sublime abodes for their patience and steadfastness, and they shall be met there with greetings and ‘Peace,’

75.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
75.text: Those shall be recompensed with the highest rank for their patience. There they shall receive a greeting, and peace!

75.author: Saheeh International
75.text: Those will be awarded the Chamber for what they patiently endured, and they will be received therein with greetings and [words of] peace.

75.author: Sarwar
75.text: They will all receive Paradise as their reward for their forbearance and patience, where they will be greeted with, "Peace be with you."

75.author: Shakir
75.text: These shall be rewarded with high places because they were patient, and shall be met therein with greetings and salutations.

75.author: Wahiduddin Khan
75.text: These are the ones who will be rewarded with lofty mansions in Paradise, for their steadfastness. They will be received therein with greetings of welcome and salutations of peace.

75.author: Yusuf Ali
75.text: Those are the ones who will be rewarded with the highest place in heaven, because of their patient constancy: therein shall they be met with salutations and peace,

75.author: Transliteration
75.text: Olaika yujzawna alghurfata bima sabaroo wayulaqqawna feeha tahiyyatan wasalaman

76.author: Ahmed Ali
76.text: And abide there for ever: What an excellent destination and abode!

76.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
76.text: They shall abide in it forever; what an excellent abode and place of stay.

76.author: Arberry
76.text: Therein they shall dwell forever; fair it is as a lodging-place and an abode.

76.author: Daryabadi
76.text: Abiders therein: excellent it is as an abode and as a station.

76.author: Hilali & Khan
76.text: Abiding therein; excellent it is as an abode, and as a place to dwell.

76.author: Itani
76.text: Abiding therein forever—it is an excellent residence and destination.

76.author: Maududi
76.text: and wherein they will live for ever: what an excellent abode and what an excellent resting place!

76.author: Mubarakpuri
76.text: Abiding therein -- excellent it is as an abode, and as a place to rest in.

76.author: Pickthall
76.text: Abiding there for ever. Happy is it as abode and station!

76.author: Qarai
76.text: to abide in them [forever], an excellent station and abode.

76.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
76.text: There they shall live for ever; a fine dwelling place, and residence.

76.author: Saheeh International
76.text: Abiding eternally therein. Good is the settlement and residence.

76.author: Sarwar
76.text: They will live therein forever, the best abode and place of rest.

76.author: Shakir
76.text: Abiding therein; goodly the abode and the resting-place.

76.author: Wahiduddin Khan
76.text: There they shall abide forever: a blessed dwelling and a blessed resting place.

76.author: Yusuf Ali
76.text: Dwelling therein;- how beautiful an abode and place of rest!

77.author: Ahmed Ali
77.text: Say: "My Lord is not concerned on your account if you do not pray to Him. You have surely done with denying; soon will come the inevitable judgement.

77.author: Ahmed Raza Khan
77.text: Say (O dear Prophet Mohammed – peace and blessings be upon him) “You have no value before My Lord if you do not worship Him; so you have denied – therefore the punishment that remains, will occur.”

77.author: Arberry
77.text: Say: 'My Lord esteems you not at all were it not for your prayer, for you have cried lies, and it shall surely be fastened.'

77.author: Daryabadi
77.text: Say thou: my Lord careth not for you were it not for your prayer. whereas ye have even belied, so presently this denial shall come as the cleaving punishment.

77.author: Hilali & Khan
77.text: Say (O Muhammad SAW to the disbelievers): "My Lord pays attention to you only because of your invocation to Him. But now you have indeed denied (Him). So the torment will be yours for ever (inseparable permanent punishment)."

77.author: Itani
77.text: Say, “What are you to my Lord without your prayers? You have denied the truth, and the inevitable will happen.”

77.author: Maududi
77.text: O Muhammad, tell the people, "MyLord does not care at all if you do not invoke Him. Now that you have denied (His Revelation), you will soon be awarded such a punishment which you will never be able to avoid."

77.author: Mubarakpuri
77.text: Say: "My Lord pays attention to you only because of your invocation to Him. But now you have indeed denied. So the torment will be yours forever."

77.author: Pickthall
77.text: Say (O Muhammad, unto the disbelievers): My Lord would not concern Himself with you but for your prayer. But now ye have denied (the Truth), therefor there will be judgment.

77.author: Qarai
77.text: Say, ‘Were it not for the sake of summoning you [to faith], what store my Lord would have set by you? But you impugned [me and my summons], so it will soon follow as a result.’

77.author: Qaribullah & Darwish
77.text: Say: 'My Lord cares little for you if it was not for your supplication, indeed you have belied (the Messenger and the Koran) so it (the punishment) will be fastened'

77.author: Saheeh International
77.text: Say, "What would my Lord care for you if not for your supplication?" For you [disbelievers] have denied, so your denial is going to be adherent.

77.author: Sarwar
77.text: (Muhammad), say (to the disbeliever) "It does not matter to my Lord whether you worship Him or not. You have rejected His guidance and your punishment is inevitable."

77.author: Shakir
77.text: Say: My Lord would not care for you were it not for your prayer; but you have indeed rejected (the truth), so that which shall cleave shall come.

77.author: Wahiduddin Khan
77.text: Say, "What would my Lord care for you, if you do not call on Him. Because you have indeed rejected the truth and His punishment is bound to overtake you.

77.author: Yusuf Ali
77.text: Say (to the Rejecters): "My Lord is not uneasy because of you if ye call not on Him: But ye have indeed rejected (Him), and soon will come the inevitable (punishment)!"

77.author: Transliteration
77.text: Qul ma yaAAbao bikum rabbee lawla duAAaokum faqad kaththabtum fasawfa yakoonu lizaman
